fields.System.Title: [FE] Search Existing Regimens by Drug Composition (Drug Name, Brand/Generic, or HCPCS)
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, or CRR, I want to search for main regimens using drug name(s) (brand or generic) or HCPCS codes , so that I can locate regimens based on their drug composition.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow the user to enter a search regimen by:
    1. HCPCS code
    2. Drug Brand Name
    3. Drug Generic Name
  2. When a search value is entered, the system should match it against drug records in the drug library using brand name, generic name, HCPCS code, and their associated relationships
  3. A regimen should be returned if it contains at least one drug that corresponds to the matched drug record from the drug library:
    1. If a brand name is entered, the system should return regimens that contain either that brand or its linked generic version.
    2. If a generic name is entered, the system should return regimens that contain either that generic or its linked brand version.
    3. If an HCPCS code is entered, the system should return regimens that contain the drug tied to that HCPCS code.
  4. Search should support partial match (fuzzy/typeahead behavior):
    1. The system shall return diagnosis-filtered regimen results when the user enters at least 3 characters of a regimen-related search term, including partial text matches on HCPCS code or drug name.
    2. The system shall support limited typo tolerance for drug-name searches only and shall rank exact HCPCS code matches and exact drug-name matches above partial or approximate matches.
  5. Search results should return regimen-level entries only (not individual drugs):
    1. If multiple drugs within a regimen match the search criteria, the regimen shall appear only once in the result set.
  6. Search Results Display:
    1. The system shall use the descriptive text inside parentheses from the Admin Portal regimen label as the preferred regimen display name
    2. If the descriptive parenthetical text cannot be extracted reliably, the system shall display the full Admin Portal regimen label as fallback and shall not show a truncated, partially parsed, or reconstructed title.
    3. the system should not show the Diagnosis Group in the result row.
    4. the system should not show protocol-status values (for example, Auto, Non-Auto, S2
      1. Example:
        1. Source Admin Portal label : 
          carboplatin, paclitaxel, pembrolizumab (Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC))
        2. Expected display: 
          Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC)
        3. Valid fallback if extraction is not reliable: 
          carboplatin, paclitaxel, pembrolizumab (Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC))
  7. Search results should be limited to regimens associated with the Diagnosis Group already selected on the case in accordance with Retrieve Diagnosis-Scoped Regimens from Regimen Library
  8. Ancillary drugs (drug type=ancillary) should not appear as standalone regimen search results.
  9. If no regimens match the search criteria within the diagnosis-scoped set, the result should be empty.
Technical considerations:
  • Paging and incremental loading should be supported.
  • Ancillary-only drugs must not appear as standalone results.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, or CRR, I want to switch between equivalent drugs within a regimen when appropriate so that I can select the correct billable product without changing the clinical intent.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. User should be able to switch between equivalent drugs based on shared RxCUI during Chemo entry.
  2. For each case-level drug the system should determine whether eligible alternative drugs exist:
    1. the system should identify drugs sharing the same ingredient RxCUI.
    2. the system should consistently apply the same alternative-identification logic to Existing regimen drugs, Custom regimen drugs, Ancillary drugs
  3. User should be able to select the alternative drug in the dosing table:
    1.  A switching control (HCPCS dropdown) should be visible/enabled only when alternative drugs exist
    2. The generic drug must be selected as the default option when alternatives exist.
  4. Alternatives must be grouped and ordered as follows:
    1. Generic drugs where Route = IV, ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    2. Generic drugs where Route = Other, (including PO/oral generics), ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    3. Brand drugs where  Drug Qualifier = 505(b)(2), ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    4. Drugs where Drug Qualifier = Biosimilar, ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
  5. When an alternative drug is selected for a case-level drug, the system should update the HCPCS code and drug description to match the selected drug.

  6. Each switch should be logged as an audit event with: 

    Previous selection, New selection, User, Timestamp
Assumptions:
  1. DrugAPI returns alternative drugs, including Route information to support sorting. Additional research is required for any other alternative-selection logic.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Create Custom Regimen on Case Entry
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want to create a Custom Chemotherapy Regimen during case entry so that I can proceed when no predefined regimen is available.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The user should be able to search for drugs in a manner consistent with the existing drug search behavior, using drug name (brand or generic) or HCPCS code
  2. The system should return matching drugs in Custom Regimen drug search results using the format [HCPCS code]  [Drug Name] ([Brand Name]) when a brand name is available.
    1. The system should exclude drugs flagged as Ancillary drugs from the search results
    2. The system should use the same display format for Medical and Pharmacy drugs, with no Pharmacy-specific variation in result presentation.
    3. The system should use the clinically recognizable drug name from the source record as the primary displayed drug name and shall append the brand name in parentheses as a qualifier.
      1. Example:
        1. Source Admin: Drug Name: 3-iodobenzylguanidine, Brand Name: Azedra [2053287], HCPCS: A9590
        2. Expected display: A9590  3-iodobenzylguanidine (Azedra)
    4. The system should start returning matching results only after the user enters at least 3 characters.
    5. The system should support partial-match search and limited typo tolerance for drug-name searches, while ranking exact matches above partial or approximate matches.
  3. The user should be able to select and add drugs for the Custom Regimen:
    1. The system should require that a Custom Regimen contain at least one drug before it can be saved.
  4. The system should add each selected drug to the case-level regimen record upon selection.
  5. For drugs added to a Custom Regimen, the system should allow switching between equivalent drugs based on shared RxCUI and should exclude drugs flagged as EADL from the eligible equivalent drug set when presenting switching options - in accordance with:
    1. Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
    2. Restrict Selection of EADL-Flagged Drugs in Regimen Drug Selection
  6. The system should not persist the Custom Regimen as a predefined regimen available for future selection in other chemo cases.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Validate Custom Regimen Against Existing Regimens
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want the system to validate a Custom Chemotherapy Regimen against predefined regimens so that I can reuse an existing standard regimen when the selected drug set already matches one.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should evaluate the Custom Regimen drug set after the user has:
    1. added drugs to the Custom Regimen
    2. removed drug(-s) from a Custom Regimen
  2. The system should compare the Custom Regimen drug set to predefined regimen drug sets:
    1. The system should limit the comparison to predefined regimens mapped to the Diagnosis Group selected for the case in accordance with Retrieve Diagnosis-Scoped Regimens from Regimen Library
  3. The system should determine a match only when the Custom Regimen drug set matches exactly to a predefined regimen drug set (set equality, not partial match).
    1. If no exact match is found, the system should allow the user to continue without additional prompts.
    2. If match is found, the system should notify the user and present the matching predefined regimen
  4. The user should be able to replace the Custom Regimen with a selected predefined regimen.
  5. The user should be able to keep the Custom Regimen and continue case entry.
  6. The system should record whether the user replaced the Custom Regimen or continued with it when an exact match is presented.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Spike: Investigate mechanism to retrieve AP Drug/Clinical Library Data
fields.System.Description:
Investigate DrugsAPI and Preferred Regimens service to define how backend services will retrieve and correlate the  required clinical data for:

    2. [V1.R1.] Main Regimen Selection
    3. [V1.R1.] Allow Custom Regimens
    4. [V1.R1.] Select Ancillary Drugs
    5. [V1.R1.] Capture Basic Dosing Details
    6. [V1.R1.] Apply Payer Drug Configurations
    7. [V1.R1.] Apply Payer Non-Preferred Drug Rules
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• The investigation should cover:

    1. ICD 10 Data:
      1. Availability of ICD-10 codes and descriptions
      2. Diagnosis Group mapping
      3. Cancer stage configuration 
      4. Treatment type applicability (Chemo / GMT / XRT)
      5. Payer-specific ICD logic (common vs payer-extended codes)
    2. Master Drug + PDL (Payer Drug Library) Overlay Model
      1. Drug identifiers (NDC / GPI / HCPCS etc.):
        1. Brand/Generic Drug Name
        2. Alternative Drugs
        3. Ancillary Drugs
      2. EADL flags (if applicable)
      3. Dosing Metadata
        1. Default dosing parameters
        2. Calculation logic (BSA, flat dose, etc.)
    3. Regimen Library
      1. Regimen → Diagnosis Group linkage
      2. Regimen drug composition

  • Expected Deliverables:

    1. ERD of the relevant data model as exposed by external services.
    2. List of the main API calls we will rely on, with a brief description of what each returns.
    3. A proposed outline of backend responsibilities and interfaces for working with this data.
    4. Documented open questions and assumptions

fields.System.Title: TECH DEBT
[BE] Capture Patient Height and Weight
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want to enter and update patient height and weight so that dosing calculations can be performed accurately.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should require the user to enter patient Height and Weight before allowing Main Regimen selection, Custom Regimen drug addition, or Ancillary Drug addition for Chemotherapy.
  2. The system should allow capture of the following case-level data in the UI:
    1. Preferred Measurement System (Required)
    2. Height (Required)
    3.  Weight (Required)
  3. The system should provide the following Preferred Measurement System options:
    1. Standard (ft/in/lb) - displayed by default
    2. Standard (in/lb)
    3. Metric (cm/kg)
  4. The system should use the selected Preferred Measurement System to control the Height input format and Weight input unit as follows:
    1. Standard (ft/in/lb): Height captured as feet and inches; Weight captured in pounds
    2. Standard (in/lb): Height captured in inches; Weight captured in pounds
    3. Metric (cm/kg): Height captured in centimeters; Weight captured in kilograms
  5. The system should convert and store normalized values in calculation-ready units as follows:
    1. Height in centimeters
    2. Weight in kilograms
  6. If Intake Method = Fax, the system should allow user to select Unknown option:
    1. Both fields can be set independently
      1. Allow entering height with unknown weight (uses default weight)
      2. Allow entering weight with unknown height (uses default height)
    2. Both unknown uses both default values
    3. Default values remain hidden from users (Backend calculation only)
  7. If Unknown option is selected, the system should:
    1. auto-populate default Height and Weight values based on Gender from eligibility data as follows:
      1. Male: Height = 76 inches; Weight = 250 lbs
      2. Female: Height = 70 inches; Weight = 210 lbs
    2. use those values for BSA calculation, Allowed Dose calculation, all downstream dependent dosing calculations
    3. persist that default Height and Weight values were used.
  8. The system should:
    1. validate Height and Weight according to the rules defined in the Patient Height & Weight  and the selected Preferred Measurement System.
    2. automatically calculate BSA in the background using normalized Height and Weight in accordance with the following formula: BSA = √((Height (cm) × Weight (kg)) / 3600). The system should not display BSA in the UI and should not allow the user to directly enter or edit BSA.
    3. use the entered Height and Weight, or system default values when Unknown is selected, to calculate the initial Allowed Dose when the regimen dosing table is first loaded in accordance with [FE] Populate initial dosing details based on selected regimen details
      1. If the user manually updates the Allowed Dose after it is initially calculated in accordance with [FE] Allow User Edits to Dosing with Guardrails, the system should preserve the user-entered value and should not replace it with a recalculated value based on Height and Weight or default Height and Weight.
  9. The system should allow users to update Preferred Measurement System, Height, and Weight at any time while the case is in Draft state:
    1. If the Preferred Measurement System is changed after Height and Weight values have been entered, the system:
      1. should not clear the values
      2. should convert the already entered values and display them in the new format
    2. When Preferred Measurement System, Height, or Weight is updated, the system should trigger recalculation of BSA and all dependent dosing calculations without page refresh.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create Workflow Repository
fields.System.Description:
Workflow setting will tell rule engine what steps exist in the workflow, their order, allowed transitions, define starting step, define final steps and to which workflow applies to which business process supported by NewUM (Prior authorizations (PA), appeals, etc.)
For v1, we're developing only PA workflow, but should construct underlying functionality so that it can be scaled to additional business processes with individual workflows in the future.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Create necessary DB structure(s)
    1. Must support:
      1. Payer Name - name of the payer workflow applies to. 
        1. Payer name "General" will be used for general workflow that should apply for all payers for certain business process
      2. Type - type of the workflow, e.g. business process workflow applies to
        1. PA for prior authorization
        2. Appeals for appeals workflow
      3. Order - integer from 0-6, used to determine which level takes precedence during rule execution. Higher number takes precedence and overrides lower level rules. 
      4. Step Name
      5. Attribute - for specific step attributes like "starting" , "final"
      6. Allowed next step - integer defining allowed steps workflow can transition to from current step
      7. Status - workflow configuration status
      8. ValidTo - date configuration is valid to
      9. ValidFrom - date configuration is valid from
    2. Feel free to add other columns necessary for technical implementation 
  2. Create APIs to enable FE to consume and manage data
    1. Read endpoint available to everyone
    2. Create & Update endpoints available only to users in OH Admin role (role name TBC)
    3. No delete endpoint
  3. Validations:
    1. Only 1 workflow with combination Payer Name+Type can be active at any given time (check ValidTo and ValidFrom dates and status)
    2. Step names must be unique within workflow 
    3. Workflow must contain exactly one starting step
    4. Workflow must contain at least one final step
    5. No records with same order value allowed within same workflow
  4. All changes have to be audited according to NFR
  5. Create default configuration to be used when there is no payer specific configuration.
    1. Set Payer Name=General
    2. Set Type=PA
    1. Set order:

Order

Step Name

     Allowed Next Step

1

      Case Intake

       Start

       2

2

      Case Entry

      3, 6

      Case Assignment

      4, 6

4

      Case Processing

      5, 6

5

      Case Finalization

      6

6

      Outbound/Outputs

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create UI Configuration Repository
fields.System.Description:
UI configuration

should have

  • Enable custom UI configuration for specific steps (e.g. case entry or case processing) to be configured distinctly (e.g. different payers require distinct mandatory data during case intake, or custom fields that are not a part of “standard” UI)
    Overall case intake and entry will be the same across users and health plans. However, if a health plan does opt into specific programs, we may require add’l information in those scenarios.

  • UI configurations should be done on step-interface level.

Examples:

  • If a health plan doesn’t want us to review inpatient chemotherapy cases, we will require place of service entry so we can distinguish between inpatient and outpatient.

  • A health plan has added on the Site of Care program, we can require them to attest to some clinical exception

Grain Hierarchy is a setting that will tell rule engine which rules have precedence in any tie-breaker logic. Grain Hierarchy is always defined as a set with 6 levels with order value determining which level takes precedence - higher Order value wins.

fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Create necessary DB structure(s)
    1. Must support:
      1. Payer Name - name of the payer configuration applies to. 
      2. Order - integer from 0-6, used to determine which level takes precedence during rule execution. Higher number takes precedence and overrides lower level rules. 
      3. Grain Level - name of the data grain (or level) configuration/rule applies to (level field in configuration and rules repositories)
        1. Allowed values: Genera, Payer Parent, Payer, Line of Business, Plan Type/Product, Employer Group 
      4. ValidTo - date configuration is valid to
      5. ValidFrom - date configuration is valid from
    2. Feel free to add other columns necessary for technical implementation 
  2. Create APIs to enable FE to consume and manage data
    1. Read endpoint available to everyone
    2. Create & Update endpoints available only to users in OH Admin role (role name TBC)
    3. No delete endpoint
  3. Validations:
    1. Only 1 configuration per payer can be active at any given time (check ValidTo and ValidFrom dates)
    2. No records with same order value allowed within same configuration set
    3. All Grain levels must be present in configuration set 
  4. All changes have to be audited according to NFR
  5. Create default configuration to be used when there is no payer specific configuration.
    1. Set Payer Name=default
    1. Set order:

      0

      General

      1

      Payer Parent

      2

      Payer

      3

fields.System.Title: [BE] Record Financial Impact (FI) for NP → Preferred Drug Change (Case Entry)
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS user I want the system to record Financial Impact data when a Non-Preferred (NP) drug is replaced with a Preferred drug during case entry, so that downstream FI reporting has the full drug and dosing context for the change.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should create an FI record only for a drug change from Non-Preferred (NP) to Preferred made through the NP exception / Change to Preferred flow during case entry.
  2. The system should create the FI record only when the NP → Preferred change is present in the final submitted case state.
  3. The system should not create an FI record for: 
    1. NP → Preferred changes reverted before submission
    2. drug changes made through the  Alternative DDL
  4. Places where this change should be recorded when - 
    1. A drug is changed in the existing regimen from the NP exception dropdown.
    2. A drug is changed in a custom regimen from the NP exception dropdown.
    3. A drug results in replacing the current regimen with another matching regimen. 
  5. The system should create one FI record per distinct NP → Preferred drug change included in the submitted case.
  6. Each FI record should store, at minimum:
    1. case drug / case context
    2. applicable NP rule ID
    3. original NP drug identifiers
    4. selected preferred drug identifiers
    5. original NP dosing snapshot
    6. preferred drug dosing snapshot
  7. If the Preferred selection results in regimen replacement, the FI record should also store the original regimen context and the final regimen context.
  8. The system should persist the FI record as a point-in-time snapshot of the submitted case and should not update it based on later library or configuration changes.

[QA] Populate initial dosing details based on selected regimen details
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CRR, I want dosing fields to auto-populate based on the selected regimen and drug source so that I can start from configured defaults and complete case entry efficiently.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria: 1. Once the Medical and Pharmacy dosing tables are rendered for Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, or Ancillary Drug, the system shall populate dosing table fields based on their defined source and population logic (see Data Dictionary):

fields.System.Title: [QA] Allow User Edits to Dosing with Guardrails
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CRR, I want to edit dosing details with validation and dependency guardrails so that I can adjust treatment safely while maintaining data integrity and calculation accuracy.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow users to enter or edit dosing values in the dosing tables for drugs added via Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, and Ancillary Drug workflows (see Data Dictionary):

    2. When an editable dosing field changes, the system should recalculate dependent calculated fields once required inputs are present
    3. If required inputs for fields calculation are missing, the system should not calculate and block case progression 
    4. If a change invalidates a dependent selection (Strength - Form mismatch), the system should clear the invalid field and require a valid selection.
    5. For fields with dropdown lists, the system should restrict user selection to the available dropdown values only.
    6. When switching HCPCS within the same ingredient, the system should update HCPCS and Description only and preserve dosing values.
  2. If a defaulted Existing Regimen field is changed, the system should indicate it differs from the default:
    1. The system should retain original default and current value.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Capture Patient Height and Weight
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want to enter and update patient height and weight so that dosing calculations can be performed accurately.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should require the user to enter patient Height and Weight before allowing Main Regimen selection, Custom Regimen drug addition, or Ancillary Drug addition for Chemotherapy.
  2. The system should allow capture of the following case-level data in the UI:
    1. Preferred Measurement System (Required)
    2. Height (Required)
    3.  Weight (Required)
  3. The system should provide the following Preferred Measurement System options:
    1. Standard (ft/in/lb) - displayed by default
    2. Standard (in/lb)
    3. Metric (cm/kg)
  4. The system should use the selected Preferred Measurement System to control the Height input format and Weight input unit as follows:
    1. Standard (ft/in/lb): Height captured as feet and inches; Weight captured in pounds
    2. Standard (in/lb): Height captured in inches; Weight captured in pounds
    3. Metric (cm/kg): Height captured in centimeters; Weight captured in kilograms
  5. The system should convert and store normalized values in calculation-ready units as follows:
    1. Height in centimeters
    2. Weight in kilograms
  6. If Intake Method = Fax, the system should allow user to select Unknown option:
    1. Both fields can be set independently
      1. Allow entering height with unknown weight (uses default weight)
      2. Allow entering weight with unknown height (uses default height)
    2. Both unknown uses both default values
    3. Default values remain hidden from users (Backend calculation only)
  7. If Unknown option is selected, the system should:
    1. auto-populate default Height and Weight values based on Gender from eligibility data as follows:
      1. Male: Height = 76 inches; Weight = 250 lbs
      2. Female: Height = 70 inches; Weight = 210 lbs
    2. use those values for BSA calculation, Allowed Dose calculation, all downstream dependent dosing calculations
    3. persist that default Height and Weight values were used.
  8. The system should:
    1. validate Height and Weight according to the rules defined in the Patient Height & Weight  and the selected Preferred Measurement System.
    2. automatically calculate BSA in the background using normalized Height and Weight in accordance with the following formula: BSA = √((Height (cm) × Weight (kg)) / 3600). The system should not display BSA in the UI and should not allow the user to directly enter or edit BSA.
    3. use the entered Height and Weight, or system default values when Unknown is selected, to calculate the initial Allowed Dose when the regimen dosing table is first loaded in accordance with [FE] Populate initial dosing details based on selected regimen details
      1. If the user manually updates the Allowed Dose after it is initially calculated in accordance with [FE] Allow User Edits to Dosing with Guardrails, the system should preserve the user-entered value and should not replace it with a recalculated value based on Height and Weight or default Height and Weight
  9. The system should allow users to update Preferred Measurement System, Height, and Weight at any time while the case is in Draft state:
    1. If the Preferred Measurement System is changed after Height and Weight values have been entered, the system:
      1. should not clear the values
      2. should convert the already entered values and display them in the new format
    2. When Preferred Measurement System, Height, or Weight is updated, the system should trigger recalculation of BSA and all dependent dosing calculations without page refresh.

[BE] Calculate Allowed Dose Using Patient Height and Weight
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want the system to calculate Allowed Dose based on patient height and weight so that dosing reflects clinical rules and payer requirements.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should calculate Allowed Dose for all Medical drugs, including Existing Regimens, Custom Regimens, and Ancillary Drugs.
  2. Calculation: The system should calculate Allowed Dose field based on Units as follows:
    1. OOS for this story: Fixed-Dose Units: If Units is mg, g, or mcg, then Allowed Dose = Dosage - will be covered by [BE] Calculate Allowed Dose Without Patient Height and Weight
    2. OOS for this story: Area Under Curve (Carboplatin) Units: If the drug is Carboplatin and Units is Area Under Curve 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, then Allowed Dose = Dosage - will be covered by [BE] Calculate Allowed Dose Without Patient Height and Weight
    3. Weight-Based Units: If Units is mg/kg, mcg/kg, or g/kg, then Allowed Dose = Dosage × Weight (kg).

    4. BSA-Based Units: If Units is mg/m2, g/m2, IU/m2, or units/m2, then: 

      Allowed Dose = Dosage × BSA (where 

      B

      SA = √((Height (cm) × Weight (kg)) / 3600)  - calculated in the scope of Capture Patient Height and Weight story )
  3. Conversion to Billable UOM: After calculating Allowed Dose based on Units, the system should normalize the result to the drug’s Billable UOM:
    1. Allowed Dose should always be expressed in the same unit as the drug’s Billable UOM and should align to the following drug dose unit conversions (mg/mcg/g): mg × 1,000 = mcg, mcg × 0.001 = mg, g × 1,000 = mg, g × 1,000,000 = mcg, mg × 0.001 = g, mcg × 0.000001 = g
  4. Rounding : After normalization to Billable UOM, the system should round Allowed Dose to two decimal places using the following rounding rules:
    1. Identify the third decimal place: Look at the digit in the thousandths place (the third digit to the right of the decimal point):
      1. Round down if the third decimal place is less than 5: If the third digit is 0, 1, 2, 3, or 4, round down by leaving the second decimal place as is. Example: 3.141 becomes 3.14 (because the third digit, 1, is less than 5)
      2. Round up if the third decimal place is 5 or greater: If the third digit is 5, 6, 7, 8, or 9, round up by adding 1 to the second decimal place. Example: 3.146 becomes 3.15 (because the third digit, 6, is greater than 5)
      3. Special case for rounding up from .999: If rounding up causes the second decimal place to increase from 9 to 10, the first decimal place also needs to increase accordingly. Example: 4.999 becomes 5.00 (because rounding up 99 to 100 causes an increment in the units place).
  5. If any required field for the applicable calculation path is missing or invalid, the system should not perform the calculation
  6. If weight or height is updated then Allowed dose is re-calculated based on the new values.

fields.System.Title: [QA] Calculate Max Billing Units per Authorization (Medical Drugs)
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want the system to calculate Max Billing Units/Auth for Medical drugs so that the authorization reflects the correct billable units.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should calculate Max Billing Units/Auth only for drug rows where Billing Method = Medical.
  2. The system should use the same Max Billing Units/Auth calculation logic for Medical drugs added via Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, and Ancillary Drug.

  3. For Max Billing Units/Auth calculation to occur, the system should have the following available: 

    Allowed Dose, Adm. Per Day, Adm. Days Per Cycle, Duration per AP/Cycle in Days, CMU BU from Admin Portal.
  4. The system should derive Cycles for this calculation as:
    1. Cycles = Duration per AP/Cycle in Days rounded up to the next whole number 
  5. The system should calculate Max Billing Units/Auth as:
    1. (Allowed Dose × Adm. Per Day × Adm. Days Per Cycle × Cycles) ÷ CMU BU.
  6. The system should round Max Billing Units/Auth to 2 decimal places using the same rounding rules defined for Allowed Dose.
  7. If any required field for the applicable calculation path is missing or invalid, the system should not perform the calculation
  8. If any required field for Max Billing Units per Authorization is updated, then it should be re-calculated based on the new values.
Considerations:
  • Allowed Dose is calculated considering patient height and weight in accordance with [BE] Calculate Allowed Dose Using Patient Height and Weight

fields.System.Title: [BE] Save Existing Regimen and Drug Records to Case
fields.System.Description: Persist the selected Main Regimen and its regimen-derived drug snapshot on the Draft case.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:

  4. Each regimen-derived case drug record should include at minimum: sourceRegimenId, sourceRegimenVersion, drugId, drugCode, drugDescription, and default dosing attributes returned by the source regimen payload.
  5. The saved data should support regimen reconstruction and downstream dosing and rule processing.
  6. Regimen data should be stored as a case snapshot and should not change if the source regimen is updated later.
  7. If a new Main Regimen is saved, the API should replace the previously saved regimen and its derived case drug records.
  8. On failure, the API should return an error and leave existing saved regimen data unchanged.

fields.System.Title: Provider - final adjustments & approval
fields.System.Description:

  • Review outstanding items — address any remaining bugs, edge cases, or feedback from previous review cycles
  • Business rule validation — confirm all provider logic and rules align with the latest requirements
  • Auditability decision — finalise and document the approach for audit logging on provider data, including:
    • Which provider actions and data changes must be audited (e.g. creation, updates, status changes, assignments)
    • Audit log structure, retention policy, and storage location
    • Who is responsible for reviewing audit trails and under what conditions
    • Compliance and regulatory requirements driving the auditability approach
  • Audit implementation review — verify the agreed auditability approach is correctly implemented and tested
  • Stakeholder review — walk through the final state with business and technical stakeholders for sign-off
  • Approval sign-off — obtain formal approval from the product owner / business lead before go-live
  • Documentation update — ensure provider logic, audit decisions, and rules are documented and up to date
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:

  • Auditability approach agreed, documented, and signed off by relevant stakeholders
  • Audit logging implemented and verified for all in-scope provider actions
  • Formal approval received from the designated stakeholder(s)

fields.System.Title: [BE] Revalidate Drug Configuration When Clinical Data Changes
fields.System.Description: As a case user, I want NewUM to revalidate drug configuration when I change case drug or dosing data so that case determinations reflect the current case content.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should trigger revalidation when the user changes drug-related or clinical/dosing data in the case.
  2. Revalidation-triggering changes should include:
    1. adding a regimen drug
    2. adding an ancillary drug
    3. removing a drug
    4. replacing/changing the regimen or protocol
    5. editing dosing data
    6. editing other clinical data that participates in drug configuration checks
  3. If no relevant drug or clinical/dosing data has changed, the system should not perform an additional configuration refresh.
  4. When revalidation runs, the system should send all drugs on the case in the API refresh request.
  6. The refresh scope should include both:
    • regimen drugs
    • ancillary drugs
  7. For V1, refresh should occur on save within the case.
  8. For V1, the system should not automatically push upstream Admin Portal / PDL configuration changes into open cases if no relevant case edits were made.
  9. Refresh results should be applied back to the case after the save-triggered call completes.

fields.System.Title: [BE/Reporting] Identify Open Cases Impacted by Upstream Configuration Changes

fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:

  4. Automatic identification / flagging may be considered in a future release.

fields.System.Title: Audit Refresh Activity and Support Supervisor Override

fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:

  3. Supervisory users should be able to override drug-level determinations.
  4. The supervisor override workflow should prevent save-triggered API refresh from automatically overwriting a valid supervisory determination.
  5. Final UX for supervisor override may be defined separately, but the functional override capability is required.

fields.System.Title: Case utilisation of Provider data
fields.System.Description:
Implement and document the utilisation of Provider data within the Case management flow. This covers how provider information is consumed, applied, and surfaced across case processing, ensuring cases correctly leverage provider data for routing, assignment, and decision-making. The work should include:

  • Integration points — identify where and how Provider data feeds into Case workflows (e.g. case creation, provider assignment, routing, validation)
  • Provider data mapping — define which provider fields and attributes are relevant to case processing and how they are mapped to case entities
  • Business rules — document rules governing how provider data influences case outcomes (e.g. provider eligibility, capacity, specialisation, status-based routing)
  • Data consumption — ensure cases correctly read and apply provider data in real time or via scheduled refresh
  • Provider status handling — define case behaviour when provider data is incomplete, inactive, or in a pending/suspended state
  • Assignment logic — document how providers are selected or assigned to cases based on provider attributes and eligibility
  • Auditability — ensure provider data used in case decisions is logged and traceable, consistent with the auditability approach agreed during the Provider final approval phase
  • Testing & validation — verify case behaviour is correct across different provider statuses, assignment scenarios, and edge cases
  • Error handling — define fallback behaviour when provider data is unavailable or conflicts arise
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• Cases correctly consume and apply provider data per defined business rules
  • Provider assignment and routing logic validated end-to-end
  • All edge cases (inactive, missing, suspended provider) handled gracefully
  • Audit trail in place for provider-driven case decisions, aligned with the agreed auditability approach
  • Integration tested across all relevant case types and provider scenarios

fields.System.Title: Define the API Contract for the /Case/Modify Endpoint
fields.System.Description:
The back-end team should define and document the API contract for the /Case/Modify endpoint, in collaboration with the client-side engineer(s). The goal is to unblock front-end development by establishing a stable, agreed-upon interface as early as possible. 

Part of this task is deciding the appropriate HTTP method and update semantics — specifically, whether the endpoint will use PUT (full replacement) or PATCH (partial update) — and documenting the rationale. The initial implementation only needs to write requested changes to the database at this point; no validation logic or rule-engine involvement are required at this stage. Any parts of the contract that are still uncertain should be clearly flagged in the documentation rather than left unresolved.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The API contract for /Case/Modify is documented and accessible to the team (e.g., in OpenAPI/Swagger, Confluence, or an agreed-upon format). 
  2. The HTTP method is determined (PUT or PATCH) and the choice is documented with a brief rationale. The contract specifies the endpoint path, request headers, request body schema, and response schema (both success and error responses), consistent with the chosen method's semantics. 
  3. The contract was reviewed and agreed upon with at least one client-side engineer before being marked final. 
  4. Any fields, behaviors, or response structures that are not yet finalized are explicitly annotated (e.g., [DRAFT], [TBD], or an equivalent marker) with a brief note on what is pending and why. 
  5. There is an initial implementation which at a minimum writes all incoming changes directly to the database with no validation or rule engine involvement.
  6. The documented contract is sufficient for a front-end engineer to begin integration work independently.

fields.System.Title: Low-level locking of cases
fields.System.Description:
As an engineer I want to have control over case concurrent updates using some optimistic concurrency approach.
Idea that each case has it own hash calculated based on its field values (be aware of EF navigation properties, ignore for now).
Then the logic is that client code read case, store in state original hash, update case fileds, recalculate hash and update in db.
Update should include some filter based on case id and original not updated hash (`where(c => c.Id == id && c.Hash == notUpdatedHash)`). That means that if filter will update case - noone changed it while current client make some updates. If filter will not find case that means that someone already updated it in between the current read and update operations.
In case of failure we need to throw an error that should be consumed and client will decide what to do.

Dev notes: it would be nice to have this logic as more close to EF DB context as it can be,  even inside `SaveChangesAsync` of entity framework.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Support Case-Level Benefit Selection for Crossover Drug Lines
fields.System.Description: As a system, I need to capture, validate, persist, and return the user-selected case benefit type for drug lines configured in PDL as Crossover, so that downstream table assignment, benefit status resolution, and submission validation can be processed correctly for the current case without modifying payer-level configuration.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When a case drug line is created and the applied PDL benefit type is Crossover, the backend should mark the line as requiring a selected case benefit type.
  2. The backend should support only Medical and Pharmacy as valid selected case benefit type values for a crossover drug line.
  3. The backend should not default the selected case benefit type for a crossover drug line.
  4. Until a valid selected case benefit type is provided, the backend should treat the crossover drug line as incomplete for:
    1. dosing table assignment;
    2. applicable benefit status resolution; and
    3. submission eligibility.
  5. When a valid selected case benefit type is received, the backend should persist it on the case drug line and return it in the case payload.
  6. The backend should use the persisted selected case benefit type to:
    6.1. assign the drug line to the Medical or Pharmacy table context; and
    6.2. determine whether medical or pharmacy benefit status should be used for downstream status derivation.
  7. If multiple crossover drug lines are added in a single transaction, the backend should track selected case benefit type independently for each crossover drug line unless a shared-selection rule is explicitly applied.
  8. If the same crossover drug appears multiple times within the same case and a shared-selection rule applies, the backend should apply the same selected case benefit type to all relevant instances.
  9. The backend should reject case submission if any crossover drug line does not have a valid persisted selected case benefit type.
  10. The selected case benefit type should be stored as case-level transactional data only and should not update or override the underlying PDL drug configuration for future cases.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Apply and Save Applicable Benefit Status for Case Drug
fields.System.Description: As the NewUM backend, I need to resolve and save the applicable benefit status for each case drug line based on the applied PDL configuration and resolved benefit type, so that current drug status can be derived consistently for existing regimen, custom regimen, and ancillary drugs throughout the lifecycle of the case.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When a case drug line is created during Chemo Entry, the backend should resolve the applicable benefit status using the applied PDL configuration and the resolved benefit type for that drug line.
  2. If the resolved benefit type is Medical, the backend should use the MedicalBenefitStatus from the applied PDL configuration. Supported MedicalBenefitStatus values should be:  OOS,  PA Required, PA Exempt
  3. If the resolved benefit type is Pharmacy, the backend should use the PharmacyBenefitStatus from the applied PDL configuration. Supported PharmacyBenefitStatus values should be: OOS, PA Required, PA Exempt, Per Formulary
  4. If the resolved benefit type is Crossover, the backend should use the benefit status corresponding to the persisted case-level selected benefit type.
    1. For V1, if the resolved applicable benefit status is Per Formulary, the backend should map it to OOS for downstream current drug status derivation.
  5. The backend should save a single resolved applicable benefit status on each case drug line.
  6. The backend should apply the same benefit status resolution and save logic to drug lines created from: existing regimens, custom regimens; and  ancillary drugs.
  7. The backend should resolve and update the saved applicable benefit status when any of the following occurs:
    1. a regimen is loaded;
    2. a drug line is added;
    3. a drug line is switched to an alternative;
    4. the selected benefit type changes for a crossover drug line.
  8. Once saved at case entry, the applicable benefit status on the case drug line should remain fixed for the duration of the case and should not be recalculated due to upstream PDL configuration changes.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Update Selected Benefit Type for Crossover Drug Lines in Draft
fields.System.Description: As the NewUM backend, I need to allow the selected benefit type for a crossover case drug line to be updated while the case is in Draft, so that the case drug line context, applicable benefit status, and derived current drug status remain consistent with the latest user selection before submission.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The backend should allow update of the selected benefit type only for case drug lines with applied PDL benefit type Crossover.
  2. The backend should allow the selected benefit type to be changed only while the case status is Draft.
  3. Supported selected benefit type values for update should be:
    1. Medical
    2. Pharmacy
  4. When the selected benefit type is updated, the backend should:
    1. save the updated selected benefit type on the case drug line;
    2. update the case drug line table context to Medical or Pharmacy;
    3. re-resolve the applicable benefit status based on the updated selected benefit type; and
    4. update the derived current drug status.
  5. If the same crossover drug appears multiple times within the same case and a shared-selection rule applies, the backend should update all applicable instances consistently.
  6. The backend should reject benefit type update if the case status is not Draft.
  7. The backend should reject benefit type update if the case drug line is not configured as Crossover.
  8. The backend should return the updated selected benefit type, table context, applicable benefit status, and current drug status in the case payload.
  9. The updated selected benefit type should be stored as case-level data only and should not update the underlying PDL configuration for future cases

fields.System.Title: Implement Initial Partial Compiler for Workflow JSON to NRules
fields.System.Description:
Context:

The rules engine requires a compilation step that transforms a Workflow JSON document into a StateMachine<> object for the Stateless library (https://github.com/dotnet-state-machine/stateless), which will drive case workflow execution. The full compiler cannot be built yet due to open questions around the JSON document's final format, its construction from hierarchical Payer/Health-Plan workflow definitions, and its storage strategy. However, enough is understood about the core structure to begin an initial, scoped implementation. This ticket delivers a partial compiler that handles a limited but well-defined subset of the workflow syntax — specifically, steps with transitions and minimal conditions only, with no actions or UI configuration.

Description:

Implement a compilation method that accepts a Workflow JsonDocument and returns a StateMachine<> that implements the described workflow in the Stateless library. For this ticket, the input document is assumed to be valid and well-formed according to the limited syntax defined below. No validation of the input document is required.

The supported syntax is:

  • A workflow contains a Steps object, where each key is a step name.
  • Each step may contain a Transitions object, where each key is the name of a target step.
  • Each transition may contain a Condition property.  The exact syntax of the Condition property is still to be determined, but for now it will be a single property name.  The Condition tests if that property on a Case object (passed in to the state machine as context when executed) is non-null.
  • The StateMachine, when executed, will use the Case object's WorkflowStep as the current state of the machine.
  • Steps, transitions, and actions beyond this structure (UI config, Actions, etc.) are out of scope for this ticket.

The following is a representative example of a workflow document in this limited syntax: 

{
  "Steps": {
    "FirstStep": {
      "Transitions": {
        "SecondStep": {
          "Condition": "<C# check some value>"
        },
        "AlternateStep": {
          "Condition": "<C# check some other value>"
        }
      }
    },
    "SecondStep": {
      "Transitions": {
        "ThirdStep": {
          "Condition": "<C# check some value>"
        }
      }
    },
    "AlternateStep": {
      "Transitions": {
        "ThirdStep": {
          "Condition": "<C# check some value>"
        }
      }
    },
    "ThirdStep": {}
  }
}

The exact representation of conditions is an open question (see below). For this ticket, assume the condition string is a single Case property reference, and the condition is true if and only if that property is non-null. This assumption should be documented clearly in code comments, with a reference to the open question to be resolved in a follow-up ticket.

Open Questions (to be resolved in refinement or research subtasks — do not block this ticket on them unless necessary):

  1. Condition format: Should conditions be raw C# expression strings, or a structured JSON predicate format (e.g. {"AND": [{"EQ": [...]}]})? A decision here may require a research subtask before the condition-evaluation portion of this ticket can be finalized.
  2. Case property references: How should condition expressions reference Case fields and nested properties? Should the compiler maintain an explicit allowlist of valid references, or introspect the Case object at compile time?
  3. Test mechanism: Should the generated rules be tested through the existing Rules Harness, or should a lightweight, isolated NRules-invoking test mechanism be created specifically for compiler output verification?
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. A compilation method exists that accepts a JsonDocument representing a workflow and returns a StateMachine<>.
  2. The compiler correctly parses the Steps object and enumerates all named steps.
  3. For each step, the compiler correctly parses the Transitions object and enumerates all named target steps.
  4. The compiler implements the transitions in in the resulting StateMachine<> such that each transition:
    • Is uniquely named or identifiable by its source step and target step (e.g. "FirstStep -> SecondStep").
    • Matches when the current workflow state corresponds to the source step.
    • Evaluates the transition's Condition value by treating it as a single Case property reference, firing when that property is non-null.
    • Fires to transition the workflow state to the target step when the condition is met.
  5. Steps with no Transitions property (i.e. terminal steps) are parsed without error and have no transitions in the StateMachine.
  6. The compiled StateMachine, when executed against a suitable case object, produces correct step transitions consistent with the example workflow document provided.
  7. The assumption made about condition format and case property reference syntax is documented clearly in code comments, with a reference to the open questions to be resolved in a follow-up ticket.
  8. Unit tests cover: a multi-step workflow with branching transitions (per the example), a workflow with a terminal step, and at least one condition evaluation that routes correctly to the expected target step based on a non-null Case property.

fields.System.Title: Update SSL certificate
Design for "Condition" elements in rules engine
fields.System.Description:
Context: The Rules configuration system supports Condition elements that define boolean logic for triggering Transitions based on Case data. Before implementation can begin, the team needs to align on a syntax for authoring these expressions. Two candidate approaches have been identified — a free-form C# expression string and a structured nested JSON format — but other designs may exist. This is a discovery and proposal ticket; implementation will be tracked separately.

Description: The ticket owner should research, gather team input, and produce a formal proposal for the Condition expression syntax. The evaluation should weigh factors including ease of authoring, validation capabilities, code generation, maintainability, and extensibility.

Known candidates to evaluate include:

  • C# expression string — e.g., caseData.Status == "Draft" && caseData.Priority > 2
    • Pro: Natural, concise, familiar to developers
    • Con: Difficult to validate structurally; requires parsing or Roslyn-style evaluation
  • Nested JSON structure — e.g., {"AND": [{"EQ": ["caseData.Status", "Open"]}]}
    • Pro: Machine-readable, validatable against a schema, easily converted to code
    • Con: More verbose; less intuitive to author by hand

The owner should also surface and consider any additional designs raised by the team.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The ticket owner has consulted with relevant team members and collected their input on the candidate syntaxes and any alternatives
  2. At least the two identified candidate approaches (C# expression string and nested JSON structure) have been formally evaluated against defined criteria (e.g., authoring ease, validation, code generation, extensibility)
  3. Any additional syntax approaches surfaced during research are documented and evaluated alongside the known candidates
  4. A written proposal is produced that recommends a specific syntax, includes a rationale, and documents the trade-offs of the alternatives considered
  5. The proposal has been shared with the team for review and any feedback has been addressed or acknowledged
  6. A follow-up implementation ticket is created (or linked) based on the accepted proposal

fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should check payer Non-Preferred (NP) rules for each affected drug when:
    1. a main regimen is selected, changed, or replaced in accordance with Select Main Regimen and Persist Regimen and Drug Records to Case
    2. a custom regimen is created or edited  in accordance with Create Custom Regimen on Case Entry
    3. an ancillary drug is added or edited in accordance with Ancillary Drug Selection
    4. a crossover drug benefit type is changed (check the affected drug line) in accordance with Change Benefit Type for Crossover Drug Before Case Submission
    5. a drug is switched to an alternative drug with the same ingredient RxCUI  in accordance with Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
  2. For main regimen selection, change, or replacement, the system should evaluate NP status for all drug lines in the regimen.
  3. For custom regimen edits, ancillary drug edits, crossover benefit-type changes, and equivalent-drug switches, the system should evaluate NP status only for the affected added/changed drug line(s).
  4. For each NP evaluation, the system should retrieve the active payer Non-Preferred rule record for the drug using: 
    1. Payer ID
    2. Major LOB (in future: line of business, product line)
    3. Drug
    4. Treatment Start Date 
  5. The system should determine whether the drug is Non-Preferred by evaluating the retrieved rule against:
    1. Billing Method
    2. Rule Type - To be used for Mandatory vs Voluntary type of NP drug
    3. Diagnosis Groups - Used to check whether this diagnosis is included or excluded for NP rules of this drug
    4. Include / Exclude Diagnosis Groups - Used to define whether diagnosis mentioned in "Diagnosis Groups" to be included or excluded while checking for preference of drugs.
    5. Stage Excluded - Used to set the stage of cancer for which NP rules will not be applicable for this drug.
    6. State of issue Excluded 
    7. Member State Excluded 
  6. If a drug meets the NP rule conditions, the system should mark the drug as Non-Preferred at the dosing level
  7. If a drug is marked as Non-Preferred, the dosing table should show an NP indicator for that drug row, and the styling should differ for Mandatory vs Voluntary.

1. The system should calculate and retain a Default Quantity per Cycle value for each Pharmacy drug row populated from an Existing Regimen using the regimen default dosing values initially loaded for that row, in accordance with Calculate Quantity per Authorization (Pharmacy  Drugs)
  2. The system should recalculate the Current Quantity per Cycle value for the drug row when user edits change any input used in the calculation, including Dosage, Strength, Adm. Per Day, Adm. Days Per Cycle, and Cycles.

fields.System.Title: Define and implement payer Treatment Type data import for NewUM
fields.System.Description:
NewUM has a dependency on payer-level Treatment Type configuration. For a given payer, the system should return only the enabled Treatment Types, as this drives Treatment Type selection, Diagnosis / ICD-10 flow, and downstream workflow behavior as per [BE] Apply Payer Restrictions on Diagnoses, [BE] Select Tx Type (Chemo vs GMT vs XRT):

  • Current analysis indicates that this payer-to-Treatment-Type mapping exists upstream in OH OADB and handled through InsuranceProviderTreatmentType. 
  • The current NewUM implementation direction is to store Treatment Types in PayerConfig, but the data sourcing and population approach is still unresolved.

Because production-ready payer Treatment Type data is not yet available in the NewUM database, a temporary workaround will be used to unblock dependent backend work: 

  • if PayerConfig exists but no imported Treatment Type data is available yet, NewUM will return TreatmentType.Chemotherapy by default for the payer
  • this temporary behavior is intended only to unblock near-term work and should be replaced once production-ready payer Treatment Type data is imported into NewUM storage

This task is for the Data Team to identify and implement the appropriate approach for importing and populating payer Treatment Type data into the NewUM database.

Scope

  1. Review available upstream sources for payer-specific Treatment Type data
  2. Confirm whether OH OADB / InsuranceProviderTreatmentType is the correct source for initial import
  3. Define the import and population approach for NewUM database storage
  4. Support population of payer Treatment Type data into NewUM-owned storage
  5. Document any assumptions, limitations, and future maintenance considerations

Supported Treatment Types (with planned releases)

  • Chemotherapy (V1.R1)
  • XRT (V1.R2)
  • GMT (V1.R2)

Acceptance Criteria:

  1. Confirm the recommended source of truth for payer Treatment Type data used by NewUM.
  2. Define the import / population approach for storing payer Treatment Type data in NewUM database storage.
  3. Document the mapping for supported Treatment Type values: Chemotherapy, XRT, GMT.
  4. Identify any source data gaps, assumptions, or dependencies affecting production-ready import.
  5. Provide or implement an approach for initial population of payer Treatment Type data into NewUM storage.
  6. Document how and when the temporary default Chemotherapy fallback can be retired once imported payer-specific data becomes available.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Clear Existing Regimen and Dosing When Diagnosis Group Changes
fields.System.Description: As CRR, CRS  user, I want the system to preserve an existing regimen and its populated dosing data when an updated diagnosis remains within the same Diagnosis Group, and clear that regimen and dosing data when the updated diagnosis changes the Diagnosis Group, so that the case cannot retain a clinically incompatible regimen.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• Pre-conditions
    1. The user has selected an ICD-10 code in accordance with -  Select ICD-10 code for Dx: 
      1. The system has derived the Diagnosis Group from the selected ICD-10.
    2. The system has filtered available existing regimens based on the derived Diagnosis Group in accordance with Retrieve Diagnosis-Scoped Regimens from Regimen Library
    3. The user has selected an existing regimen from the filtered regimen list.
    4. The system has populated downstream regimen data, including dosing data, based on the selected existing regimen.

    Acceptance Criteria

    1. When the user updates the selected ICD-10, the system should derive the Diagnosis Group from the updated ICD-10 mapping.
    2. If the updated ICD-10 maps to the same Diagnosis Group as the previously selected ICD-10, the system should preserve:
      • the selected existing regimen
      • populated dosing data
      • other regimen-dependent data tied to that regimen selection
    3. If the updated ICD-10 maps to a different Diagnosis Group than the previously selected ICD-10, the system should clear:
      • the selected existing regimen
      • populated dosing data
      • other downstream regimen-dependent data tied to the previous regimen selection
    4. After clearing regimen-related data due to Diagnosis Group change, the system should require the user to select a new existing regimen from the regimen list filtered for the updated Diagnosis Group before proceeding to regimen-dependent workflow steps.
    5. Regimen reset / preservation logic should be based on the derived Diagnosis Group, not on raw ICD-10 equality.

    Post-conditions

    1. If the updated ICD-10 remains within the same Diagnosis Group, the case retains the previously selected existing regimen and its downstream populated data.
    2. If the updated ICD-10 results in a different Diagnosis Group, the case no longer contains the previously selected existing regimen or its downstream populated data.
    3. Following a Diagnosis Group change, only regimens valid for the newly derived Diagnosis Group are available for selection.

fields.System.Title: Display RxCUI tied to NDC for Alternative Pharmacy Drugs in Chemo dosing tables
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS, I want the system to display the RxCUI tied to NDC below the selected drug value when reviewing or selecting pharmacy alternatives, so that I can identify the actual pharmacy drug code used behind the scenes when HCPCS alone is not specific enough.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. This behavior should apply only for case-level drugs that support alternative drug selection through the HCPCS alternative dropdown.
  2. For those rows, when the currently selected drug is a pharmacy drug, the system should display a secondary read-only value below the selected alternative drug display in the same row.
  3. The secondary value should be the resolved RxCUI tied to NDC for the currently selected pharmacy alternative.
  4. The primary selected drug display should remain the HCPCS-based alternative display; the RxCUI tied to NDC should appear as a separate secondary line below it.
  5. This should apply wherever alternative drug switching is supported and the selected drug is billed under Pharmacy, including:
    1. Existing Regimen drugs  - Main Regimen Selection Feature
    2. Custom Regimen drugs - Allow Custom Regimen Feature
    3. Ancillary drugs - Select Ancillary Drugs Feature
  6. This should not apply to:
    1. drug rows billed under Medical
    2. drug rows where no alternative drug selection control is available
  7. The secondary code should be read-only and should not be editable or selectable by the user.
  8. When the user selects a different pharmacy alternative, the system should update the displayed RxCUI tied to NDC to match the newly selected alternative.
  9. If the resolved pharmacy code depends on additional selections such as strength and/or form, the system should not display the RxCUI tied to NDC until a valid combination is selected for the chosen alternative.
  10. If the user changes strength or form and the previously resolved pharmacy code is no longer valid, the system should clear the displayed value and display the updated RxCUI tied to NDC only after the new valid combination is resolved.
  11. The system should not expose raw representative NDC as a separate user-visible editable field.

fields.System.Title: Enforce Product Line Intake Scope Rule
fields.System.Description: As a Case Review Specialist, I want the system to evaluate whether a member’s product line is allowed for intake when a case is submitted, so that cases associated with unsupported product lines are created and then closed as out of scope before review begins
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Preconditions

  • An Intake Coordinator has searched for a member and the member was found, in accordance with https://oncologyanalytics.visualstudio.com/newUM/_workitems/edit/179603/?view=edit
  • A draft case exists for the member.
  • Patient demographic information and eligibility data for the member is available from the eligibility file or can be retrieved, in accordance with https://oncologyanalytics.visualstudio.com/newUM/_workitems/edit/179607/?view=edit
  • Eligibility verification has completed successfully and the member is determined to be eligible, in accordance with
  • https://oncologyanalytics.visualstudio.com/newUM/_workitems/edit/179611/?view=edit
  • The Intake Coordinator initiates Submit Case.


Acceptance Criteria
  • The rule is evaluated as part of Submit Case processing.
  • The rule applies only when the member is determined to be eligible.
  • The member’s product line (Line of Business) from eligibility data is compared against the payer’s configured intake scope.
    • If the member’s product line is not enabled in payer configuration:
      • The case can be submitted.

      • Upon submission, t

        he case status is updated to Out of Scope.
      • The case is administratively closed in accordance with intake scope rules.
    • If the member’s product line is enabled in payer configuration:
      • The case can be submitted.

      • Upon submission, t

        he case status is updated to Ready for Assignment.

Postconditions

  • When the product line is out of scope:

    • A case record exists containing:

      • Patient demographic information
      • Eligibility information 
    • The case is in a closed Out of Scope state.
    • The closure reason reflects a product line intake scope restriction.

    • The following are recorded and available for audit:

      • Rule evaluation outcome
      • Case status transition
      • Closure reason

fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want to search for a member using strict, validated search field combinations, so that I can identify the correct member to start case entry, while only seeing members for the health plan(s) I’m authorized to access.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must provide a Member Search capability prior to new case creation
  2. The system must uniquely identify exactly one member entity prior to allowing progression in Case Entry.
    1. For R1, all authenticated users have access to all configured Health Plans.

  3. Search execution must require one of the following combinations:

    1. Health Plan + Date of Birth + Member ID
    2. Health Plan + Date of Birth + First Name + Last Name
    3. Health Plan + Date of Birth + Member ID + First Name + Last Name
  4. The system must validate all search fields in accordance with the Data Dictionary and block execution if validation fails.
  5. The system must enforce payer-specific configuration for Member ID for the selected Health Plan, including:
    1. Max Member ID Length (Required in Payer Configs)
    2. Member ID Expression / Pattern (Optional in Payer Configs)
    3. Member ID Message  (Optional in Payer Configs)
  6. The system must execute the search strictly within the selected Health Plan 
  7. Matching must be exact; when multiple identifiers are provided, all identifiers must match the same member entity.
  8. The system must return Found when exactly one distinct member entity matches the criteria.
  9. The system must return Not Found when no member matches the criteria
    1. Edge cases related to Member Search
  10. The system must log each search attempt

Technical Considerations:
  1. The eligibility ingestion and ETL pipeline are out of scope for NF Data team. Member Search consumes eligibility data as provided by the OH data team.
  2. Search must return a single logical member entity
  3. No identity reconciliation or record merging is performed in R1.
  4. Configs (from OADB) are out of scope for this story > stub for fetching the configs

fields.System.Title: [BE] View/Edit Member Information Form for Found Member (core eligibility data + optional eligibility attributes )
fields.System.Description:
As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to retrieve and present member information after a member is found,
So that I can review demographic, contact, eligibility, and basic treatment context before proceeding with case entry decisions.

Story Scope / Boundaries:
  • Triggered when Member Search returns Found (single logical member)
  • Retrieves member -related data associated with the identified member
  • Makes member information available for review and limited modification where permitted
  • Does not create a draft case
  • Does not perform eligibility-at-time-of-service validation or scope determination (handled by subsequent stories)
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must render the Member Information form after a successful Member Search result (Found).
  2. The form must display member information organized into logical groups as defined in the Data Dictionary:
    1. Demographic information
    2. Member  contact information
    3. Member representative information (when available)
    4. Eligibility information - all required fields
    5. Basic treatment information (limited to treatment start date)
  3. All eligibility-derived member information must be displayed as read-only.
  4. The system must not allow editing, correction, or override of eligibility-derived data in R1.
  5. Treatment Start Date must be displayed as empty by default:
    1. The system must allow the user to enter or modify Treatment Start Date.
  6. Displaying the Member Information form must not create, persist, or update a case record.
  7. If required eligibility attributes (as defined in the Data Dictionary) are missing, the system must prevent progression to downstream steps and display a blocking message - see Edge Cases for Display Member Information
  8. If optional eligibility attributes (as defined in the Data Dictionary) are missing, then:

    1. op

      tional attributes are not displayed or displayed blank per UI design
    2. do not block progression

fields.System.Title: Display Member Information Form When Multiple Matches Are Returned
