fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When Member Search identifies a member, the system displays the Member Information form once, even if multiple eligibility records exist for that member.
  2. The system represents the member as a single member entity, with eligibility information reflecting one or more associated eligibility records.
  3. The system does not require the user to select a specific eligibility record at this stage.
  4. Eligibility details are displayed to provide visibility only; no eligibility prioritization or selection occurs at this step.
  5. The user may proceed to enter the Treatment Start Date and trigger eligibility verification.

Considerations

  •  UI/UX for displaying multiple eligibility records
  • Preferred model: one member with multiple eligibility records behind it.
    • Continuous coverage periods should be treated as a single coverage span where possible; non-continuous periods remain separate.
    • If a Health Plan–provided member identifier is not available (legacy plans), the system may not be able to reliably group eligibility records; behavior in such cases must be defined (TBD).
  • No eligibility filtering or resolution is performed in this story.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Validate Member Eligibility at Treatment Start Date
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to validate that the member has active coverage on the treatment start date, so that only eligible cases can proceed.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Eligibility verification may be initiated only after:
    1. A member has been successfully identified, and
    2. Patient Information has been saved with a Treatment Start Date.
  2. Treatment Start Date is the authoritative reference date for eligibility verification.

  3. Upon initiation, the system must evaluate whether the member has coverage active on the Treatment Start Date using: 

    Coverage Effective Date, Coverage Expiration Date
  4. An eligibility record is considered active if:
    1. Treatment Start Date is greater than or equal to Coverage Effective Date, and
    2. Treatment Start Date is less than or equal to Coverage Expiration Date
  5. If eligibility record is active on the Treatment Start Date, the system must return outcome: Eligible.
  6. If no eligibility record is active on the Treatment Start Date, the system must return outcome: Not Eligible.

  7. If the outcome is Not Eligible, the system must:

    1. Prevent progression to scope determination, and
    2. Block further progression in the case entry flow
    3. Edge Cases – Eligibility Verification
  8. The system records the eligibility verification outcome and the eligibility record(s) evaluated for audit.

fields.System.Title: Determine Member Scope (In-Scope vs Carve-Out)
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to determine whether an eligible member request is in scope for OncoHealth review, so that only appropriate cases proceed to case creation and review.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system determines whether the eligible member/request is In Scope or Out of Scope (Carve-Out) for OncoHealth review.
  2. Scope evaluation uses eligibility attributes received from the Health Plan.

  3. The system evaluates the following eligibility fields:

    1. Delegation indicator
    2. Employer Group ID
    3. Line of Business
    4. Coverage Product / Plan Code
  4. If any eligibility field indicates non-delegation or carve-out, the member is classified as Out of Scope.
  5. If no carve-out condition is met, the member is classified as In Scope.
  6. Scope evaluation is Health Plan–specific and uses payer configuration defined in Admin Portal.
  7. Scope determination occurs before case submission and before clinical workflows.
    1. If In Scope, the user may proceed to draft case creation.
    1. If Out of Scope, the system blocks progression to standard case creation and returns an Out-of-Scope outcome.

Considerations:
  1. Define scope determination rules and coverage attributes, such as: Line of Business, Product Line / Product Offering, Employer Group / Benefit Package, State of Plan Issue

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create Draft Case and Save Progress
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want to create a draft case and save my progress during case entry, so that I can either pause and resume later or continue directly with diagnosis and treatment selection.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must allow Draft Case creation once a member has been successfully identified and the Member Information form is rendered in accordance with:
    1. Search for a member using required inputs (guardrails)
    2. View/Edit Member Information Form for Found Member (core eligibility data): 
      1. Treatment Start Date must be displayed as empty by default:
        1. The system must allow the user to enter or modify Treatment Start Date.

        2. Draft Case creation must not require: 

          Eligibility verification and Scope determination

  2. The system must provide two actions:

    1. Save as Draft and remain on the current step.
    2. Save as Draft and proceed to the next step:
      1. Eligibility verification is required

  3. Upon Draft Case creation, the system must:

    1. Generate and assign a unique Case Identifier.
    2. Persist all data captured to date.
    3. Set Case Status to Draft.
    4. Record Created Date/Time and Created By.
  4. The system must allow saving the Draft throughout case entry.
  5. The user must be able to retrieve and resume the Draft Case at any time.
  6. All Draft Case creation and updates must be logged for audit.
Considerations:
  1. Case State Model > Draft:
    1. Created after member selection.
    2. May contain incomplete data.
    3. Eligibility and scope may be pending or not evaluated.
    4. User may edit and save repeatedly.

fields.System.Title: View Eligibility History
Override Eligibility
Attach Documents
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want to attach supporting documents during case entry, so that relevant materials are available when the case is reviewed.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The user can upload one or more documents during Case Entry.
  2. Documents can be uploaded before or after a draft case is created.
  3. Allowed file types and size limits (per file and per case/session) are enforced at upload time.
  4. Multiple files can be uploaded in a single action.
  5. Each document must have a Document Type selected from a configured list to be considered successfully attached.
  6. On successful upload, the system displays document metadata:
    1. name, type, uploaded date/time, uploaded by, status
  7. Documents uploaded before draft creation are automatically linked once the draft case exists.
  8. Only authorized users can view or download attached documents.
  9. If upload fails, the document is not attached and the user is informed.
  10. Upload and access actions are logged for audit.
Technical Considerations
  • File type and size constraints are enforced consistently.
  • Multi-file and large uploads are supported without partial persistence.
  • Document files are stored in secure object storage; metadata is stored in the application database.
  • Document Type list is sourced from TBD
  • Document access is enforced by role, case visibility, and health plan context.
  • Files are encrypted in transit and at rest.

fields.System.Title: Testing UX User Story
fields.System.Description:
Link to wireframes:
https://figma.com/file/abc456/UX-Wireframes-Q1?node-id=2345:6789

User story:
As a customer completing a purchase, I need to select my preferred payment method so that I can pay for my order securely and conveniently.

Flow context:
This is step 2 of 3 in the checkout flow:

  1. Shipping address
  2. Payment method (this screen)
  3. Order review

User arrives here after completing shipping address. After selecting payment and entering details, they proceed to order review.

Key interactions:

  • User can choose between saved payment methods or add a new one
  • Saved cards show last 4 digits and card brand icon
  • "Add new payment method" expands inline form below saved methods
  • CVV required for saved cards (security best practice)
  • Default payment method is pre-selected
  • "Continue to review" button disabled until valid payment selected

Open questions:

  • Should we support PayPal/Apple Pay on this screen or handle those separately?
  • Do we show billing address on this screen or assume it matches shipping?
  • Should users be able to edit saved payment methods here or only in account settings?
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• High-fidelity design matches wireframe structure and user flow
  • All interactive states defined (hover, focus, disabled, error)
  • Empty state designed (no saved payment methods)
  • Error states designed (invalid card, declined card, expired card)
  • Design system components used where available
  • Accessibility annotations included
  • Developer handoff documentation complete

fields.System.Title: Draw ERD for Regimen and Person
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:

fields.System.Title: [BE] Load ICD-10 Library with Diagnosis Group
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, CSS, I want the system to load ICD-10 codes for Dx selection, so that I can select a valid diagnosis that is consistent with centralized clinical configuration.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system shall retrieve ICD-10 records from NewUM reference data populated via the migration pipeline.

  2. For each ICD-10 record, the system shall load:

    1. ICD-10 Code
    2. ICD-10 Description
    3. Diagnosis Group
    4. Treatment Type Applicability (e.g., Chemo, GMT, XRT) 
    5. Payer Applicability (All or specific Payers) - in accordance with [BE] Apply Payer Restrictions on Diagnoses
    6. Active Status: The system shall return only ICD-10 records where Active Status = true.

  3. Diagnosis Group shall be system-derived based on ICD-10 mapping and shall not be editable in NewUM.
  4. The system shall persist the ICD reference data version identifier and load timestamp at case creation.
  5. The system shall record the reference data version or timestamp used for diagnosis retrieval for audit purposes.

  6. If ICD reference data is unavailable:

    1. Diagnosis selection shall be blocked.
    2. A recoverable system error shall be displayed.
    3. The failure shall be logged.
Technical Considerations
  • Data contract must guarantee non-null Diagnosis Group for each ICD-10.
  • Check whether ICD-10 records contain: Active Status flag, Effective Start / End Dates
  • TBD: Spike to investigate data source: Drugs API and Preferred Regimens:
    • Dx group
    • Treatment Type Applicability (e.g., Chemo, GMT, XRT)
    • Payer Applicability (All or specific Payers)

fields.System.Title: [FE] Select ICD-10 code for Dx
fields.System.Description: As a CRS , I want to search and select an ICD-10 diagnosis, So that the case contains the required diagnosis for prior authorization.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow the user to search ICD-10 codes by:
    1. ICD-10 code (exact or partial)
    2. ICD-10 description (keyword)
    3. Data Dictionary
  2. The system shall return only ICD-10 records that are mapped to the active Diagnosis Group context
  3. The system shall display, for each ICD-10 search result:
    1. ICD-10 code
    2. ICD-10 description
  4. The system shall allow the user to select exactly one ICD-10 from the search results.
  5. The system shall prevent progression to the next workflow step until an ICD-10 is selected.
  6. Upon ICD-10 selection, the system shall persist on the case: ICD-10 code, ICD-10 description, Diagnosis Group identifier and name
  7. If the selected ICD-10 does not have a valid Diagnosis Group mapping, the system shall:
    1. Block selection
    2. Display an error message

Technical Considerations
  • Diagnosis Group is system-derived from the ICD-10 mapping and is not user-editable.
  • Diagnosis Group (not the raw ICD-10 code) is used as the primary input for regimen search downstream.
  • Treatment Type filtering and payer-specific diagnosis restrictions are enforced by separate stories and consume applicability attributes loaded via reference data.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Select Cancer Stage for Diagnosis
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, Provider Staff, or Health Plan User, I want to select a cancer stage relevant to the chosen diagnosis, So that reviewers have appropriate clinical context during evaluation.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should require an ICD-10 diagnosis to be selected before a Cancer Stage value can be entered:
    1. Data Dictionary
  2. The system should limit Cancer Stage options to values relevant to the selected diagnosis (Diagnosis Group).
  3. The system should require Cancer Stage selection before the user can proceed to the next workflow step.
  4. The system should allow selection of exactly one Cancer Stage value.
  5. The system should save the selected Cancer Stage on the case.
  6. If the user changes the selected ICD-10 diagnosis, the system should clear the previously selected Cancer Stage.
  7. After ICD-10 change, the system should require Cancer Stage to be selected again before proceeding.

fields.System.Title: Prototype interface between rules engine and case queuing system

The rules engine should not actually control the queuing; that functionality is too strongly coupled to UI/UX decisions.

fields.System.Title: Prototype custom field handling in rules engine

To get configuration for now, make a stub to pull custom fields from configuration.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:

fields.System.Title: Audit Logging for Databricks Jobs & Notebooks
fields.System.Description:
Databricks Jobs provide minimal built-in logging (run status only). We need custom audit tables to track executions, errors, and data quality results for operational visibility and troubleshooting.

Acceptance Criteria

  • audit schema created in Databricks catalog
  • execution_log table tracks: execution ID, pipeline name, notebook path, job/run ID, status, record counts, start/end time, duration
  • error_log table tracks: execution ID, error type, error message, stack trace, affected table, severity
  • data_quality_log table tracks: execution ID, table name, check name, total/passed/failed counts
  • Reusable Python logging utility created (log start, success, failure, data quality)
  • Utility re-raises exceptions so Jobs still show as failed in Databricks UI
  • One existing pipeline instrumented as proof of concept

Subtasks

  1. Create audit schema with appropriate permissions
  2. Create execution_log table (Delta, partitioned by date)
  3. Create error_log table
  4. Create data_quality_log table
  5. Build reusable Python logging utility module
  6. Instrument one existing pipeline as proof of concept

fields.System.Title: [SPIKE] Research Logging Capabilities of Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines (DLT)
fields.System.Description:
Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines (formerly DLT) may provide built-in logging that reduces or eliminates the need for custom audit tables. Before adopting DLT for future pipelines, we need to understand what it logs automatically, what gaps remain, and whether our custom audit schema would need to be adapted.

Questions to Answer

  • What does the DLT event log capture automatically? (row counts, errors, quality metrics, lineage)
  • How do we query the DLT event log? Document reusable SQL queries.
  • What business/compliance context is NOT captured by DLT?
  • Would we still need custom audit tables? If so, what would a simplified version look like?
  • What is the cost difference between DLT and standard Jobs for our workloads?
  • Can DLT run in triggered (batch) mode on a nightly schedule?

Output

A short findings document with recommendations on whether to adopt DLT and what additional logging would be needed.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Retrieve Diagnosis-Scoped Regimens from Regimen Library
fields.System.Description: As a  CRS , I want to retrieve only regimens associated with the selected Diagnosis Group so that only clinically relevant regimens are available for selection.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should retrieve regimens using the Diagnosis Group stored on the case.
    1. Regimen retrieval should assume and Treatment Type were completed in prior workflow steps and should not introduce additional blocking Diagnosis logic at this stage.
  2. The system should return only regimens mapped to the case’s Diagnosis Group:
    1. The system should return only active/published regimens
  3. The regimen record should include sufficient metadata - check Data Dictionary
  4. The system should ensure the result set contains no duplicate regimen entries.
  5. If no regimens meet the defined criteria, the system should return an empty result set.
Technical Considerations:
  1. Diagnosis Group must already be stored on the case prior to retrieval in accordance with Select ICD-10 (Filtered by Diagnosis Group)
  2. For the same Diagnosis Group and system date, the system shall return a consistent and repeatable set of regimens, applying the same filtering rules and ordering logic.
Assumption:
  1. DrugAPI can retrieve regimens, including associated Regimen Drugs and Alternative Drugs
  2. Additional validation is required to confirm that all required attributes and behaviors align with NewUM functional and data requirements - TBD will be covered by  [Data] Spike: Admin Portal (AP) Drug API Coverage & Clinical Data Model Validation for NewUM R1 (ICD-10, Drugs, Regimens)

fields.System.Title: [BE] Search Existing Regimens by Drug Composition (Drug Name, Brand/Generic, or HCPCS)
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, or CRR, I want to search for main regimens using drug name(s) (brand or generic) or HCPCS codes , so that I can locate regimens based on their drug composition.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow the user to enter a search regimen by:
    1. HCPCS code
    2. Drug Brand Name
    3. Drug Generic Name
  2. When a search value is entered, the system should match it against drug records in the drug library using brand name, generic name, HCPCS code, and their associated relationships
  3. A regimen should be returned if it contains at least one drug that corresponds to the matched drug record from the drug library:
    1. If a brand name is entered, the system should return regimens that contain either that brand or its linked generic version.
    2. If a generic name is entered, the system should return regimens that contain either that generic or its linked brand version.
    3. If an HCPCS code is entered, the system should return regimens that contain the drug tied to that HCPCS code.
  4. Search should support partial match (fuzzy/typeahead behavior):
    1. The system shall return diagnosis-filtered regimen results when the user enters at least 3 characters of a regimen-related search term, including partial text matches on HCPCS code or drug name.
    2. The system shall support limited typo tolerance for drug-name searches only and shall rank exact HCPCS code matches and exact drug-name matches above partial or approximate matches.
  5. Search results should return regimen-level entries only (not individual drugs):
    1. If multiple drugs within a regimen match the search criteria, the regimen shall appear only once in the result set.
  6. Search Results Display:
    1. The system shall use the descriptive text inside parentheses from the Admin Portal regimen label as the preferred regimen display name
    2. If the descriptive parenthetical text cannot be extracted reliably, the system shall display the full Admin Portal regimen label as fallback and shall not show a truncated, partially parsed, or reconstructed title.
    3. the system should not show the Diagnosis Group in the result row.
    4. the system should not show protocol-status values (for example, Auto, Non-Auto, S2
      1. Example:
        1. Source Admin Portal label : 
          carboplatin, paclitaxel, pembrolizumab (Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC))
        2. Expected display: 
          Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC)
        3. Valid fallback if extraction is not reliable: 
          carboplatin, paclitaxel, pembrolizumab (Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC))
  7. Search results should be limited to regimens associated with the Diagnosis Group already selected on the case in accordance with Retrieve Diagnosis-Scoped Regimens from Regimen Library
  8. Ancillary drugs (drug type=ancillary) should not appear as standalone regimen search results.
  9. If no regimens match the search criteria within the diagnosis-scoped set, the result should be empty.
Technical considerations:
  • Paging and incremental loading should be supported.
  • Ancillary-only drugs must not appear as standalone results.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Select Existing Regimen
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff (Provider Portal), CRS, or CRR, I want to select a main regimen and have all associated regimen and drug details saved to the case so that the case reflects the selected treatment plan at the time of submission.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow the user to select one Main Regimen from the regimen search results implemented in accordance with Search Regimens by Drug Composition (Drug Name, Brand/Generic, or HCPCS)

  2. Upon selection, the system should retrieve the regimen configuration, including: 

    1. Regimen metadata
    2. Associated drug composition:
      1. drug ID
      2. drug code
      3. drug description
    3. Default dosing-related attributes required to initialize the case:

  3. Upon selection, the regimen should be visibly associated with the case in Draft status.
  4. Upon Save, the system should  record the selected regimen and the associated case-level drug entries derived from it sufficient to:
    1. Identify the selected regimen and version
    2. Reconstruct the regimen structure
    3. Support downstream dosing, billing, and rule processing
  5. If Save fails:
    1. The system should display an error message.
    2. The selected regimen should remain visible in the UI until the user takes further action.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, or CRR, I want to switch between equivalent drugs within a regimen when appropriate so that I can select the correct billable product without changing the clinical intent.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. User should be able to switch between equivalent drugs based on shared RxCUI during Chemo entry.
  2. For each case-level drug the system should determine whether eligible alternative drugs exist:
    1. the system should identify drugs sharing the same ingredient RxCUI.
    2. the system should consistently apply the same alternative-identification logic to Existing regimen drugs, Custom regimen drugs, Ancillary drugs
  3. User should be able to select the alternative drug in the dosing table:
    1.  A switching control (HCPCS dropdown) should be visible/enabled only when alternative drugs exist
    2. The generic drug must be selected as the default option when alternatives exist.
  4. Alternatives must be grouped and ordered as follows:
    1. Generic drugs where Route = IV, ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    2. Generic drugs where Route = Other, (including PO/oral generics), ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    3. Brand drugs where  Drug Qualifier = 505(b)(2), ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    4. Drugs where Drug Qualifier = Biosimilar, ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
  5. When an alternative drug is selected for a case-level drug, the system should update the HCPCS code and drug description to match the selected drug.
  6. For each case-level drug, the system should check the EADL flag for all alternative drugs identified
    1. The system should remove any drug flagged as EADL from the alternative drug list, even if it shares the same RxCUI.
    2. After EADL filtering is applied, the system should determine whether switching is allowed based on the remaining alternatives.
  7. If no eligible alternatives remain after removing EADL drugs, the switching control should be hidden or disabled for that case-level drug.

  8. Each switch should be logged as an audit event with: 

    Previous selection, New selection, User, Timestamp
Assumptions:
  1. DrugAPI returns alternative drugs, including Route information to support sorting. Additional research is required for any other alternative-selection logic.

fields.System.Title: [QA] Restrict Selection of EADL-Flagged Drugs in Regimen Drug Selection
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, or CRR, I want clinically inappropriate drugs excluded from alternative options so that only valid equivalents are presented during switching.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. For each case-level drug, the system should check the EADL flag for all alternative drugs identified based on shared RxCUI in accordance with Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
  2. The system should remove any drug flagged as EADL from the alternative drug list, even if it shares the same RxCUI.
  3. After EADL filtering is applied, the system should determine whether switching is allowed based on the remaining alternatives.
  4. If no eligible alternatives remain after removing EADL drugs, the switching control should be hidden or disabled for that case-level drug.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Separate Medical and Pharmacy Drugs into Distinct Dosing Tables
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, or CRR, I want regimen drugs to be automatically separated into Medical and Pharmacy dosing tables so that I can review and manage dosing information accurately based on how the drug is administered and billed.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When the user selects Chemotherapy and adds drugs via Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, or Ancillary Drug, the system shall retrieve the Benefit type for each drug from the Payer Drug Library (PDL) in accordance with Apply Benefit Type Configuration from PDL (Medical vs Pharmacy Drug)

    1. Table placement shall be driven exclusively by the Benefit Type value from the PDL 

  2. After drugs are added to the case, the system shall automatically render two dosing tables based on the Benefit Type classification.

    1. Drugs with Benefit Type = Medical shall be displayed in the Medical Dosing Table:

      1. Medical table column set (see Data Dictionary fields where Applies To (Benefit Type) contains Medical.

    2. Drugs with Benefit Type = Pharmacy shall be displayed in the Pharmacy Dosing Table:

      1. Pharmacy table column set (see Data Dictionary fields where Applies To (Benefit Type) contains Pharmacy.
    3. Drugs configured with Benefit Type = Crossover should require explicit benefit type selection (Medical or Pharmacy) before placement in a dosing table. The crossover selection and assignment logic is out of scope for this story and  will be covered by : 
      1. Require Benefit Selection for Crossover Drugs
      2. Change Benefit Type for Crossover Drugs
  3. If both Medical and Pharmacy drugs are present, both dosing tables shall be displayed simultaneously.
  4. If only one benefit type is present, only the corresponding dosing table shall be displayed.
  5. A drug should appear in only one dosing table at any time.
  6. The dosing tables shall render immediately after regimen selection, custom regimen drug addition, or ancillary drug addition without requiring page refresh

fields.System.Title: [FE] Filter ICD-10 Diagnoses by Treatment Type Applicability (GMT / XRT)
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS selecting a diagnosis, I want ICD-10 search results to be filtered based on the selected Treatment Type (Chemo, GMT, or XRT), so that I can only select diagnoses that are clinically applicable to that treatment.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should apply Treatment Type applicability rules when returning ICD-10 search results.
  2. Treatment Type applicability should be determined from the Request Type configuration for the ICD-10 diagnosis in the Admin Portal.

  3. The system should return only ICD-10 codes that:

    1. Are mapped to the selected Diagnosis Group, and
    2. Are configured as applicable to the selected Treatment Type.
  4. When Treatment Type = Chemo, the system should include ICD-10 codes where the configured Request Type contains Chemo.
  5. When Treatment Type = GMT, the system should include ICD-10 codes where the configured Request Type contains GMT.
  6. When Treatment Type = XRT, the system should include ICD-10 codes where the configured Request Type contains XRT.
  7. If an ICD-10 diagnosis is configured with all Request Type values selected, the system should treat it as applicable to all treatment types.
  8. The system should return only ICD-10 codes with Active Status = true.
Considerations:
  • Treatment Type applicability should be sourced from the Request Type column in the Admin Portal ICD-10 Diagnosis Groups configuration.
  • If all Request Type options are selected, the system should interpret the diagnosis as applicable to all treatment types.
  • For V1.R1, Treatment Type will be Chemo, therefore only diagnoses configured with Chemo in Request Type should be returned.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Populate initial dosing details based on selected regimen details
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CRR, I want dosing fields to auto-populate based on the selected regimen and drug source so that I can start from configured defaults and complete case entry efficiently.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria: 1. Once the Medical and Pharmacy dosing tables are rendered for Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, or Ancillary Drug, the system shall populate dosing table fields based on their defined source and population logic (see Data Dictionary):

fields.System.Title: [QA] Calculate Max Billing Units per Authorization (Medical Drugs)
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want the system to calculate Max Billing Units/Auth for Medical drugs so that the authorization reflects the correct billable units.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should calculate Max Billing Units/Auth only for drug rows where Billing Method = Medical.
  2. The system should use the same Max Billing Units/Auth calculation logic for Medical drugs added via Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, and Ancillary Drug.

  3. For Max Billing Units/Auth calculation to occur, the system should have the following available: 

    Allowed Dose, Adm. Per Day, Adm. Days Per Cycle, Duration per AP/Cycle in Days, CMU BU from Admin Portal.
  4. The system should derive Cycles for this calculation as:
    1. Cycles = Duration per AP/Cycle in Days rounded up to the next whole number 
  5. The system should calculate Max Billing Units/Auth as:
    1. (Allowed Dose × Adm. Per Day × Adm. Days Per Cycle × Cycles) ÷ CMU BU.
  6. The system should round Max Billing Units/Auth to 2 decimal places using the same rounding rules defined for Allowed Dose.
  7. If any required field for the applicable calculation path is missing or invalid, the system should not perform the calculation
  8. If any required field for Max Billing Units per Authorization is updated, then it should be re-calculated based on the new values.
Considerations:
  • Allowed Dose is calculated w/o patient height and weight consideration  in accordance with [BE] Calculate Allowed Dose Without Patient Height and Weight

fields.System.Title: [QA] Select Drug Strength and Form for Pharmacy Drugs
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want to select Drug Strength and Form for Pharmacy drugs so that the correct NDC-linked identifiers are selected and Quantity per Auth can be calculated accurately.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should display Strength and Form fields only for drug rows where Billing Method = Pharmacy.
  2. The system should set Strength and Form as blank by default and require both fields to be selected before the Pharmacy drug row can be saved or submitted (see Data Dictionary)
    1. Strength:
      1. Users should be able to select a Strength value from the Strength dropdown.
      2. The system should populate the Strength dropdown using all Strength values available under the drug’s Representative NDC records in the Master Drug Library.

    2. Form: 
      1. Users should be able to select a Form value from the Strength dropdown.
      2. The system should populate the Form dropdown using all RxNorm Dose Form values available under the drug’s Representative NDC records in the Master Drug Library.
    3. Fields interdependency: 
      1. The system should determine valid Strength and Form combinations based on Representative NDC mappings in the Master Drug Library.

      3. When the user selects a Strength value, the system should filter the Form dropdown to only the Form values valid for the selected Strength.
      4. When the user selects a Form value, the system should filter the Strength dropdown to only the Strength values valid for the selected Form.
      5. Example (based on Fields Interdependency image attached): 
        1. If the user selects Strength = 70 mg/ml, the system should filter the Form dropdown to display only Oral Suspension
        2. If the user selects Strength = 140 mg, the system should filter the Form dropdown to display only Oral Capsule and Oral Tablet
        3. If the user selects Form = Oral Capsule, the system should filter the Strength dropdown to display only Strength values that are mapped to Oral Capsule in the Representative NDC records - 70 mg, 140 mg
  3. If only one valid Strength or Form value exists either on initial render or after filtering, the system should auto-populate that field.
  4. If the user changes a previously selected Strength or Form, the system should re-evaluate valid combinations and clear the other field if the existing value is no longer valid.
  5. Once both Strength and Form are selected, the system should persist the corresponding RxCUI tied to NDC, Representative NDC, and GPI. Example:
    1. Example (based on Fields Interdependency image attached): 
      1. If the user selects 140 mg Strength AND Oral Capsule Form, the system should persist the following values for the drug row: 
        1. RxCUI Tied to NDC = 1442992
        2. Representative NDC = 57962014009
        3. GPI = 21532133000120
  6. The same field behavior, validation rules, and dropdown population logic should apply to Pharmacy drugs added via Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, and Ancillary Drug.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Calculate Allowed Dose Without Patient Height and Weight
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want the system to calculate Allowed Dose so that dosing reflects clinical rules and payer requirements.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should calculate Allowed Dose for all Medical drugs, including Existing Regimens, Custom Regimens, and Ancillary Drugs.
  2. Calculation: The system should calculate Allowed Dose field on Units as follows:
    1. Fixed-Dose Units: If Units is mg, g, or mcg, then Allowed Dose = Dosage.
    2. Area Under Curve (Carboplatin) Units: If the drug is Carboplatin and Units is Area Under Curve 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, then Allowed Dose = Dosage.
    3. OOS for this story : Weight-Based Units: If Units is mg/kg, mcg/kg, or g/kg, then Allowed Dose = Dosage × Weight (kg) - will be covered by [BE] Calculate Allowed Dose Using Patient Height and Weight

    4. OOS for this story: BSA-Based Units: If Units is mg/m2, g/m2, IU/m2, or units/m2, then: 

      Allowed Dose = Dosage × BSA (where 

      B

      SA = √((Height (cm) × Weight (kg)) / 3600)  - calculated in the scope of Capture Patient Height and Weight story ) - will be covered by [BE] Calculate Allowed Dose Using Patient Height and Weight
  3. Conversion to Billable UOM: After calculating Allowed Dose based on Units, the system should normalize the result to the drug’s Billable UOM:
    1. Allowed Dose should always be expressed in the same unit as the drug’s Billable UOM and should align to the following drug dose unit conversions (mg/mcg/g): mg × 1,000 = mcg, mcg × 0.001 = mg, g × 1,000 = mg, g × 1,000,000 = mcg, mg × 0.001 = g, mcg × 0.000001 = g
  4. Rounding : After normalization to Billable UOM, the system should round Allowed Dose to two decimal places using the following rounding rules:
    1. Identify the third decimal place: Look at the digit in the thousandths place (the third digit to the right of the decimal point):
      1. Round down if the third decimal place is less than 5: If the third digit is 0, 1, 2, 3, or 4, round down by leaving the second decimal place as is. Example: 3.141 becomes 3.14 (because the third digit, 1, is less than 5)
      2. Round up if the third decimal place is 5 or greater: If the third digit is 5, 6, 7, 8, or 9, round up by adding 1 to the second decimal place. Example: 3.146 becomes 3.15 (because the third digit, 6, is greater than 5)
      3. Special case for rounding up from .999: If rounding up causes the second decimal place to increase from 9 to 10, the first decimal place also needs to increase accordingly. Example: 4.999 becomes 5.00 (because rounding up 99 to 100 causes an increment in the units place).
  5. If any required field for the applicable calculation path is missing or invalid, the system should not perform the calculation
  6. If weight or height is updated then Allowed dose is re-calculated based on the new values.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Allow User Edits to Dosing with Guardrails
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CRR, I want to edit dosing details with validation and dependency guardrails so that I can adjust treatment safely while maintaining data integrity and calculation accuracy.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow users to enter or edit dosing values in the dosing tables for drugs added via Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, and Ancillary Drug workflows (see Data Dictionary):

    2. When an editable dosing field changes, the system should recalculate dependent calculated fields once required inputs are present
    3. If required inputs for fields calculation are missing, the system should not calculate and block case progression 
    4. If a change invalidates a dependent selection (Strength - Form mismatch), the system should clear the invalid field and require a valid selection.
    5. For fields with dropdown lists, the system should restrict user selection to the available dropdown values only.
    6. When switching HCPCS within the same ingredient, the system should update HCPCS and Description only and preserve dosing values.
  2. If a defaulted Existing Regimen field is changed, the system should indicate it differs from the default:
    1. The system should retain original default and current value.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Indicate When Max Billing Units/Auth Exceeds Default
fields.System.Description: As a CRS/CRR user, I want the system to flag Medical drug rows when user-entered dosing changes cause Max Billing Units/Auth to exceed the default value, so that exceptions to the original regimen-derived dosing can be reviewed.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should calculate and retain a Default Max Billing Units/Auth value for each Medical drug row populated from an Existing Regimen using the regimen default dosing values initially loaded for that row (as per  Calculate Max Billing Units per Authorization )
  2. The system should recalculate the Current Max Billing Units/Auth value for the drug row when user edits change any input used in the calculation, including Allowed Dose , Adm. Per Day ,  Adm. Days Per Cycle , Cycles
  3. The system should compare the Current Max Billing Units/Auth value to the retained Default Max Billing Units/Auth value for the same drug row and mark the drug row as Exceeds Default when Current Max Billing Units/Auth > Default Max Billing Units/Auth:
    1. the system should indicate when Calculated Medical Billing Units Exceed Default

    2. the system should display both values available for the affected drug row:

      1. Default Max Billing Units/Auth
      2. Current Max Billing Units/Auth

  4. The system should allow the user to enter an exceed reason (optional):

    1. The system should store the exceed reason at the individual case-drug-row level.
  5. The system should remove the Exceeds Default status automatically when the Current Max Billing Units/Auth value becomes less than or equal to the Default Max Billing Units/Auth value.
  6. The Exceeds Default evaluation should be performed independently for each drug row and should not affect other drug rows on the case.
  7. If either the Default Max Billing Units/Auth value or the Current Max Billing Units/Auth value cannot be calculated, the system should not evaluate Exceeds Default for that row.
  8. When the user proceeds with a Medical drug row that remains in Exceeds Default status, the system should create a log record to capture, at minimum, the case identifier, drug row identifier, drug identifier/HCPCS, Default Max Billing Units/Auth, Current Max Billing Units/Auth, optional exceed reason, user identifier, and action timestamp.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Search Drugs for Custom Regimen on Case Entry
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want to create a Custom Chemotherapy Regimen during case entry so that I can proceed when no predefined regimen is available.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The user should be able to search for drugs in a manner consistent with the existing drug search behavior implemented in [BE] Search Existing Regimens by Drug Composition (Drug Name, Brand/Generic, or HCPCS) using:
    1. HCPCS code
    2. Drug Brand Name
    3. Drug Generic Name
  2. The system should return matching drugs in Custom Regimen drug search results using the format [HCPCS code]  [Drug Name] ([Brand Name]) when a brand name is available.
    1. The system should exclude drugs flagged as Ancillary drugs from the search results
    2. The system should use the same display format for Medical and Pharmacy drugs, with no Pharmacy-specific variation in result presentation.
    3. The system should use the clinically recognizable drug name from the source record as the primary displayed drug name and shall append the brand name in parentheses as a qualifier.
      1. Example:
        1. Source Admin: Drug Name: 3-iodobenzylguanidine, Brand Name: Azedra [2053287], HCPCS: A9590
        2. Expected display: A9590 3-iodobenzylguanidine (Azedra)
    4. The system should start returning matching results only after the user enters at least 3 characters.
    5. The system should sort the search results in ascending alphabetical order
  3. The user should be able to search and select multiple drugs for the Custom Regimen:
    1. The system should require that a Custom Regimen contain at least one drug before it can be saved.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Enter Dosing Information for Custom Regimen
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want to enter dosing information for drugs in a Custom Chemotherapy Regimen so that the case contains complete and validated clinical dosing details prior to submission.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Upon Custom Regimen creation and drug addition, the following logic should be applied consistently across Custom Regimens, predefined Regimens, and Ancillary Drugs:
    1. Render dosing tables based on the resolved Billing Method  in accordance with Separate Medical and Pharmacy Drugs into Distinct Dosing Tables 
    2. Allow switching between equivalent drug alternatives, if available in accordance with:
      1. Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
      2. Restrict Selection of EADL-Flagged Drugs in Regimen Drug Selection
    3. for Pharmacy Drugs:
      1. Calculate Pharmacy Quantity per Authorization 
      2. Select Drug Strength and Form
    4. For Medical Drugs:
      1. Calculate Medical Billing Units 
      2. Calculate Allowed Dose 
  2. Upon rendering the dosing table(s), the system should:
    1. Populate the HCPCS code and drug name for each drug added to the Custom Regimen.
    2. Leave all dosing input fields blank by default.
  3. User should be able to enter values in all editable dosing fields:
    1. System-calculated fields and status fields should not be edited
    2. Dosing guardrails, validation rules, and calculation logic shall be applied consistently across Custom Regimens, predefined Regimens, and Ancillary Drugs in accordance with Allow User Edits to Dosing with Guardrails

fields.System.Title: Restrict Custom Regimen Creation for External Users
fields.System.Description: As a system, I must prevent external requesting providers from creating Custom Chemotherapy Regimens to ensure treatment submissions adhere to established evidence-based regimens and standardized oncology utilization management rules.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should resolve the authenticated user’s role and effective permissions and make them available to the chemotherapy case workflow.
  2. The system should apply the user’s effective role and permissions before rendering regimen selection functionality.
  3. The system should prevent Custom Regimen creation for users classified as External, including scenarios where no predefined regimens are returned from regimen search.
Considerations
  • Role-based access control must be enforced server-side 
  • User role should be resolved from the authenticated identity token at request time.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Validate Custom Regimen Against Existing Regimens
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want the system to validate a Custom Chemotherapy Regimen against predefined regimens so that I can reuse an existing standard regimen when the selected drug set already matches one.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should evaluate the Custom Regimen drug set after the user has:
    1. added drugs to the Custom Regimen
    2. removed drug(-s) from a Custom Regimen
  2. The system should compare the Custom Regimen drug set to predefined regimen drug sets:
    1. The system should limit the comparison to predefined regimens mapped to the Diagnosis Group selected for the case in accordance with Retrieve Diagnosis-Scoped Regimens from Regimen Library
  3. The system should determine a match only when the Custom Regimen drug set matches exactly to a predefined regimen drug set
    1. If no exact match is found, the system should allow the user to continue without additional prompts.
    2. If match is found, the system should notify the user and present the matching predefined regimen
      1. The search results should be displayed in accordance with Search Existing Regimens by Drug Composition (Drug Name, Brand/Generic, or HCPCS) and sorted alphabetically in ascending order (A–Z).
  4. The user should be able to replace the Custom Regimen with a selected predefined regimen.
  5. The user should be able to keep the Custom Regimen and continue case entry.
  6. The system should record whether the user replaced the Custom Regimen or continued with it when an exact match is presented.

fields.System.Title: Pend Custom Regimens for Auto-Rules
fields.System.Description: As a system, I must prevent Custom Chemotherapy Regimens from being auto-approved so that non-standard treatment combinations undergo clinical review before authorization.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Upon case submission, if the selected regimen is classified as a Custom Regimen, the system shall mark the regimen as not eligible for auto-approval.
  2. The system shall route all Custom Regimen cases to Clinical Review.
  3. The system shall record “Custom Regimen” as an exception reason in the protocol evaluation results.
    1. If additional exception scenarios are triggered (e.g., non-preferred drug, OON provider, quantity limits exceeded), the system shall capture and display all applicable exception reasons.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Add Ancillary Drugs to Chemo
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want to search and add ancillary drugs during chemo entry so that supportive therapy drugs can be included in the request.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The user should be able to access the ancillary drug search during chemo entry.
    1. Ancillary drug search should be available regardless of whether a main regimen has been selected.
    2. Ancillary drug search should remain available while case state = Draft.
    3. Only drugs designated as Ancillary (Drug Type) should be available for selection.
  2. The user should be able to search for drugs in a manner consistent with the existing drug search behavior, using drug name (brand or generic) or HCPCS code
  3. The system should return matching Ancillary Drugs using the format: [Clinical Category]: [HCPCS code] [Generic Name] ([Brand Name]) when a brand name is available.
  4. The system should use the same result structure across all Ancillary Drug search results
    1. Example:
      1. Source Admin: 
        1. Clinical Category = Bone-Modifying Agents
        2. Drug Generic Name =  denosumab 
        3. Drug Brand Name: Xbryk
        4. HCPCS = Q5159 
      2. Expected display: "Bone-Modifying Agents: Q5159 denosumab (Xbryk)"
  5. The system should start returning matching results only after the user enters at least 3 characters.
  6. The system should support partial-match search and limited typo tolerance for drug-name searches, while ranking exact matches above partial or approximate matches.
  7. The user should be able to add multiple ancillary drugs within the same Clinical Category:
    1. The system should not allow duplicate ancillary drugs to be added within the case.
    2. If no results match the search criteria, the system should display a “No results found” message.
  8. Upon selection, the system should associate the selected ancillary drug to the case in accordance with [BE] Save Selected Ancillary Drug to Case
Considerations:
  1. Clinical Category for Ancillary Drugs is Admin Portal :

fields.System.Title: [FE] Restrict Ancillary Drug Selection by Clinical Category
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want the system to restrict ancillary drug selection by  diagnosis group so that users cannot select disallowed ancillary combinations.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When the user adds an ancillary drug, the system should check the drug’s ClinicalCategoryId against ancillary drugs already associated to the case.
  2. The system should allow only one ancillary drug per ClinicalCategoryId by default:
    1. the only exception is Nausea which should allow multiple selections.
  3. If the user attempts to add more than one ancillary drug in a Clinical Category that allows only one, the system should block the action and display a validation message.
  4. The system should validate Clinical Category  rules at the time a drug is added.
  5. If the user removes an ancillary drug from a Clinical Category , the system should allow another drug from that same group to be added if the maximum limit is no longer exceeded.
  6. The system should not allow case Save or Submit if any Clinical Category  contains more drugs than allowed.
  7. Clinical Category  rules should apply whether or not a main regimen exists on the case.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create Ancillary-Only Chemotherapy Requests
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want to create a chemotherapy request that contains only ancillary drugs so that supportive-therapy-only requests can be submitted without selecting a main regimen.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The user should be able to associate ancillary drugs to the case without selecting a main regimen.
  2. The system should allow the case to remain without a MainRegimenId when only ancillary drugs are selected.
  3. The user should be able to save the case in Draft status when at least one ancillary drug is associated.
  4. The system should allow Save when:
    1. No main regimen is selected, and
    2. At least one ancillary drug is associated, and
    3. All required validations pass.
  5. All ancillary drugs should be stored as individual case-level drug records.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Align Ancillary Drug Cycle Fields with Main Regimen
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want ancillary drug Cycles and Cycles in Days to default from the selected Main Regimen when appropriate so that dosing consistency is maintained without overriding intentional user input or creating incorrect assumptions.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should apply Main Regimen alignment only to ancillary drugs in the following Clinical Categories:
    1. Antiemetics
    2. Long-Acting GCSFs
    3. Short-Acting GCSFs
  2. The system should evaluate the following ancillary fields independently:
    1. Administration Days per Cycle
    2. Cycles
    3. Cycles in Days
  3. The system should treat a Main Regimen value as qualifying for a given field only when:
    1. the Main Treatment is an Existing Regimen; AND
    2. all Main Regimen drug lines have the same default value for that field.
  4. For each in-scope field, the system should apply the following defaulting order:
    1. first, the qualifying Main Regimen value, when available;
    2. otherwise, the ancillary drug default from Admin Portal, when available;
    3. otherwise, blank.
  5. If a field was system-populated from an ancillary Admin Portal default and a qualifying Main Regimen is later added, the system should update that field to match the Main Regimen.
  6. If a field is currently system-aligned to the Main Regimen and the corresponding Main Regimen value later changes to a new qualifying shared value, the system should update the ancillary field accordingly.
  7. If a field has been manually entered or modified by the user, the system should not overwrite that value.
  8. If a field is currently system-aligned to the Main Regimen and the corresponding Main Regimen field later no longer has a qualifying shared value, the system should retain the current ancillary value.
  9. The system should allow the user to edit Administration Days per Cycle, Cycles, Cycles in Days in accordance with  Data Dictionary
  10. The system should support the following scenarios: Ancillary Cycles Scenarios

fields.System.Title: [Dynamic Cycle Calculations] Calculate dynamic cycles based on requested treatment end date
[Dispense As Written (DAW)] Control Brand vs Generic Drug Selection During Case Entry
fields.System.Description:
Provide a 'dispense as written' option during case entry
System prioritizes selection of the generic drug over the branded version in drug selection

fields.System.Title: [BE] Apply Benefit Type Configuration from PDL (Medical vs Pharmacy Drug)
fields.System.Description: As a CSR/CRR, I want the system to apply the configured benefit type from PDL to each drug line added during Chemo Entry based on the member’s eligibility, so that each drug line is placed into the correct dosing table and benefit routing is consistent.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should retrieve the active PDL configuration for each drug line added during Chemo Entry (existing regimen, custom regimen, ancillary) using the member’s Insurance Provider and Major LOB:
    1. The system should retrieve the PDL record that is effective for the service date - Treatment Start Date
  2. The system should place a drug line in the Medical dosing table when the configured benefit type is Medical, and the user should not be able to modify the benefit type.
  3. The system should place a drug line in the Pharmacy dosing table when the configured benefit type is Pharmacy, and the user should not be able to modify the benefit type.
  4. The system should apply the same benefit type logic consistently for existing regimen drugs, custom regimen drugs, and ancillary drugs.
  5. The system should save the resolved benefit type on each case drug line at the time the drug line is created
  6. The system should retain the applied benefit type for the duration of the case even if upstream PDL configuration changes after case entry.

Considerations
  • PDL configuration should be retrieved using eligibility-derived Insurance Provider and Major LOB:
    • Current state: line of business is returning null in the Drug API because it is not yet configured; Will require changes in OH Admin Portal.
    • Future roadmap: expand granularity beyond line of business (e.g., product line, employer group) while keeping the system scalable. Will require changes in OH Admin Portal.
  • The system retrieve the PDL record that is effective for the service date - Treatment Start Date
  • The system should store the applied configured benefit type at the drug-line level in the case data model.
  • The system should implement snapshot behavior so applied benefit type does not change mid-case if PDL is updated upstream.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Display drug benefit status (OOS, PA Required, PA Exempt)
fields.System.Description: As a CSR/CRR, I want the system to apply medical and pharmacy benefit status values from PDL for each drug line added during Chemo Entry and derive the current drug status shown in the dosing tables, so that I can understand whether each drug is out of scope, PA exempt, or requires review
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When a drug is added during Chemo Entry, including through an existing regimen, custom regimen, or ancillary drug selection, and the benefit type for that drug has been determined in accordance with Apply Payer Drug Configurations | Apply Benefit Type from PDL , the system should determine the applicable benefit status for the drug line based on the benefit type.
  2. If the benefit type is Medical, the system should use the Medical benefit status configured in the PDL for that drug:
    1. In the PDL, the Medical benefit status for a drug may be configured as one of the following values: OOS / PA Required / PA Exempt
    2. When the drug is added to the case, the system should resolve and save a single applicable Medical benefit status for that drug line.
  3. If the benefit type is Pharmacy, the system should use the Pharmacy benefit status configured in the PDL for that drug.
    1. In the PDL, the Pharmacy benefit status for a drug may be configured as one of the following values: OOS / PA Required / PA Exempt / Per Formulary, which should map to OOS for now
    2. When the drug is added to the case, the system should resolve and save a single applicable Pharmacy benefit status for that drug line.
  4. If the benefit type is Crossover, the system should use the benefit status corresponding to the user-selected benefit type, Medical or Pharmacy.
  5. The system should derive the current drug status for display from the applicable benefit status in accordance with Apply Payer Drug Configurations | Drug Status Determination Logic
  6. The resulting current drug status should be displayed in the dosing table during case entry and after save or submission.
  7. The benefit status configuration applied at case entry should remain fixed for the duration of the case, even if the upstream configuration changes.
  8. The same benefit status logic should apply consistently to existing regimen drugs, custom regimen drugs, and ancillary drugs.
  9. The system should derive, or re-derive, the current drug status immediately when any of the following events occur:
    1. a regimen is loaded in accordance with Main Regimen Selection
    2. a drug is added in accordance with Allow Custom Regimensand Select Ancillary Drugs
    3. a drug is switched to an alternative in accordance with Main Regimen Selection | Allow Switching Between Equivalent Regimen Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
    4. the selected benefit type changes for a crossover drug line in accordance with Apply Payer Drug Configurations | Change Benefit Type for Crossover Drug Before Case Submission

Previously validated designs (may change for NewUM)

fields.System.Title: [FE] Require Benefit Selection for Crossover Drugs
fields.System.Description: As a CSR/CRR, I want the system to require benefit selection for crossover drug lines during Chemo Entry, so that each crossover drug line is placed into the correct dosing table and the correct benefit status rules are applied.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When a drug configured in the PDL as Crossover is added during Chemo Entry, including through an existing regimen, custom regimen, or ancillary drug selection, the system should require the user to select a benefit type of Medical or Pharmacy before the drug line is placed into a dosing table.
    1. The system should prevent a crossover drug line from being placed into either dosing table until a benefit type of Medical or Pharmacy is selected.
    2. The system should not assign a default benefit type for crossover drugs..
  2. If multiple crossover drugs are added simultaneously, such as through a regimen, the system should require a benefit selection for each crossover drug line.
  3. If the same crossover drug appears multiple times within a regimen, the selected benefit type should be applied consistently to all instances of that drug within the case.
  4. The selected benefit type should determine:
    1. whether the Medical or Pharmacy benefit type context is used to derive the applicable benefit status in accordance with Apply Payer Drug Configurations | Apply benefit status from PDL during Chemo Entry
    2. whether the drug line is displayed in the Medical or Pharmacy dosing table;
  5. The system should prevent case save and submission if any crossover drug line does not have a selected benefit type.
  6. The selected benefit type should apply only to the current case and should not modify the PDL classification of the drug for future cases

fields.System.Title: [FE] Apply NP Drug Rules
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS user I want the system to evaluate payer Non-Preferred (NP) drug rules when drugs are selected or updated, so that I can immediately see NP status
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should check payer Non-Preferred (NP) rules for each affected drug when:
    1. a main regimen is selected, changed, or replaced in accordance with Select Main Regimen and Persist Regimen and Drug Records to Case
    2. a custom regimen is created or edited  in accordance with Create Custom Regimen on Case Entry
    3. an ancillary drug is added or edited in accordance with Ancillary Drug Selection
    4. a crossover drug benefit type is changed (check the affected drug line) in accordance with Change Benefit Type for Crossover Drug Before Case Submission
    5. a drug is switched to an alternative drug with the same ingredient RxCUI  in accordance with Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
  2. For main regimen selection, change, or replacement, the system should evaluate NP status for all drug lines in the regimen.
  3. For custom regimen edits, ancillary drug edits, crossover benefit-type changes, and equivalent-drug switches, the system should evaluate NP status only for the affected added/changed drug line(s).
  4. For each NP evaluation, the system should retrieve the active payer Non-Preferred rule record for the drug using: 
    1. Payer ID
    2. Major LOB (in future: line of business, product line)
    3. Drug
    4. Treatment Start Date 
  5. The system should determine whether the drug is Non-Preferred by evaluating the retrieved rule against:
    1. Billing Method
    2. Rule Type - To be used for Mandatory vs Voluntary type of NP drug
    3. Diagnosis Groups - Used to check whether this diagnosis is included or excluded for NP rules of this drug
    4. Include / Exclude Diagnosis Groups - Used to define whether diagnosis mentioned in "Diagnosis Groups" to be included or excluded while checking for preference of drugs.
    5. Stage Excluded - Used to set the stage of cancer for which NP rules will not be applicable for this drug.
    6. State of issue Excluded 
    7. Member State Excluded 
  6. If a drug meets the NP rule conditions, the system should mark the drug as Non-Preferred at the dosing level
  7. If a drug is marked as Non-Preferred, the dosing table should show an NP indicator for that drug row, and the styling should differ for Mandatory vs Voluntary.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Allow User to Change to Preferred Drug
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS user I want to replace a Mandatory Non-Preferred drug with a payer Preferred drug, so that I can proceed with case creation
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When a Non-Preferred (NP) drug is present during case entry (in accordance with Apply NP Drug Rules Based on Member Eligibility (incl. NP Drug Label Display at Dosing Level)), the system should allow the user to change that NP drug to a Preferred drug:
    1. If the case contains at least one drug line marked as NP, the system should display the Change to Preferred action
    2. The Change to Preferred Agent action should be available only for drug lines currently marked as NP and should use the Preferred alternatives displayed for that NP drug in accordance with Display Preferred Drug Options to User
  3. The user should be able to select a Preferred drug from the displayed Preferred alternatives for that NP drug:
    1. If the selected Preferred drug is available in the Alternative Drug list (in accordance with Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI), the system should:
      1. replace the NP drug with the Preferred drug while keeping the regimen unchanged
      2. remove the NP indicator for that drug line while the change is unsaved
    2. If the selected Preferred drug is not available in the Alternative Drug list, the system should:
      1. identify existing regimen options that match the updated drug combination (selected Preferred drug plus the other drugs from the current regimen) using the matching-regimen logic implemented in Validate Custom Regimen Against Existing Regimens
      2. allow the user to select one of the matching regimen options and update the dosing tables accordingly
    3. If no matching existing regimen exists for the selected Preferred drug, the system should:
      1. notify the user that no matching regimen exists
      2. return the user to the NP drug state with no change applied
  4. Until case submission,
    1. if a Preferred selection is reverted for a drug line that was changed through direct replacement within the current regimen, the system should:
      1. restore the last saved NP drug for that drug line
      2. restore NP status and NP-related actions for that drug line
      3. keep the rest of the current regimen unchanged.
    2. if a Preferred selection is reverted for a case that was changed through matching-regimen replacement, the system should:
      1. restore the last saved regimen that existed before the matching regimen was applied
      2. restore the last saved drug lines and NP statuses associated with that prior regimen
      3. discard the unsaved or interim regimen-replacement state.
  5. After case submission,
    1. if a direct NP-to-Preferred change was made, the selected Preferred drug should persist on that drug line unless reverted before case submission.
    2. if a matching-regimen selection was made, the selected regimen should persist as the current regimen unless reverted before case submission.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Capture Clinical Exception Reason for NP Drug
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS user I want to document an exception reason when I keep a Non-Preferred drug (NP rule type = Voluntary ), so that the case can continue and the clinical justification is stored on the drug.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. If a drug line is flagged as Non-Preferred (NP), the system should allow an Exception Reason to be recorded for that NP drug line:
    1. Exception Reason should be optional 
    2. Exception Reason should be selected from a predefined static list.
  2. The system should store the Exception Reason at the drug-line level, with one Exception Reason per NP drug line.
  3. If the selected Exception Reason requires supporting text, including Other, the system should require the supporting text before save.
  4. The system should persist the selected Exception Reason and any supporting text on the NP drug line within the case record.
  5. If one or more NP drug lines remain on the case at Save and any NP drug line does not have an Exception Reason, the system should allow the user to proceed without an Exception Reason.
  6. The system should allow user to clear a previously saved or unsaved Exception Reason before save.
  7. Upon save, if an NP drug line remains on the case with an Exception Reason, the saved Exception Reason should remain associated with that NP drug line for the current case state.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Display Preferred Drug Options to User
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS user I want to see the payer Preferred drug options for a Non-Preferred drug, so that I can select an approved alternative in the same dosing context.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. If the case contains at least one drug line marked as Non-Preferred (NP), the system should display the Change to Preferred action (Optional)
  3. When the user initiates Change to Preferred, the system should display all current NP drug lines on the case in their current case order.
  4. For each NP drug line, the system should display the available Preferred drug alternatives returned for that NP drug line.

  5. For a given NP drug line, Preferred drug alternatives should be displayed in the following order:

    1. Preferred drugs available in the Alternative Drug list
    2. Preferred drugs not available in the Alternative Drug list.
  6. If the user selects a Preferred drug that is available in the Alternative Drug list, the system should proceed with the direct replacement flow for that drug line.
  7. If the user selects a Preferred drug that is not available in the Alternative Drug list, the system should proceed with the matching-regimen flow for that drug line.
  8. If matching regimens are found for the selected Preferred drug, the system should display the matching regimen options and allow the user to select one.
  9. If no matching regimen is found for the selected Preferred drug, the system should notify the user and return that drug line to the original NP state with no Preferred change applied.
  10. If no Preferred drug alternatives are available for an NP drug line, the system should continue to display that NP drug line in the NP drug set, but should not display selectable Preferred drug alternatives for that line.
  11. After a successful change to a Preferred drug, the system should no longer display Preferred drug alternatives for that resolved drug line while the Preferred selection remains active.
  12. If a resolved drug line is returned to NP state, the system should display the Preferred drug alternatives for that drug line again.
  13. Until case submission, if a drug line was changed from NP to Preferred, the system should display the Revert to non-preferred action for that resolved drug line while the Preferred selection remains active.

fields.System.Title: Testing: Design system "Component name - Alert "
fields.System.Description:
This is a test ticket - do not pick up. 
Component name: Alert

Link to handover file:
https://figma.com/file/abc123/Product-Work?node-id=1234:5678

Context:
This component appeared across multiple screens in the new user onboarding flow and the account settings area. We're using alerts to communicate system messages, errors, warnings, and success states. Currently every designer is building these ad hoc, and they're inconsistent in style and behavior.

States and variants covered:

  • Types: info, success, warning, error
  • Sizes: small, medium, large
  • With/without icon
  • With/without close button
  • With/without action button
  • States: default, hover (on close button), focus (on close/action buttons)

Token dependencies:

  • Using existing color tokens for background and border
  • Using existing typography tokens
  • Using existing spacing tokens
  • New token needed: alert-icon-size (currently hardcoded at 20px)

Open questions:

  • Should we support custom icons or stick to a predefined set per alert type?
  • Do we need a "dismissible" variant that shows a close button, or should close buttons be optional on all alerts?
  • Should the action button be a text link or a button component
Assigned to: @Andrii Zhdan
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• Component covers all states and variants listed above
  • No hardcoded values, all tokens applied
  • Accessible: color contrast, keyboard navigation, ARIA labels
  • Team reviewed and approved
  • Published to design system library
  • Dev ticket created

fields.System.Title: WF: Intake - 1 Search Patient
fields.System.Description:
Link to PRD:
https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5078581249/Member+Identification+and+Coverage

Link to dev handoff wireframes:

https://www.figma.com/design/uENaQVxy3I1js8Eq9v1l0N/CASE-ENTRY-Start-a-Prior-Auth?node-id=1-3&t=1LXeHIx1qpvkMrUj-1

User story:
As a CRR about to intake a case into the system, I need to accurately identify the correct member at the start of Case Entry to ensure Oncology UM intake is initiated for the intended individual, prevent HIPAA violations, and avoid downstream rework caused by incorrect member selection.

Flow context:
This is step 1 of 5 in the Intake flow: 

  1. Search for Patient
  2. Confirm Patient Info
    1. Confirm Patient Info
    2. Confirm Eligibility #1 
  3. Add Diagnosis
  4. Attach Clinical notes & documents 
  5. Add Complete Prover Info & Eligibility Check #2 

This is the very first step a user takes to intake/enter a case. 

Key interactions:

  • User can search
  • User can select a patient that gets returned
  • Once selected, the view/layout should accommodate a patient header that includes key
      • Data:  Name, DOB, Insurer, 
      • Statuses/Alerts: Eligibility Status, Case Status, TAT
    • Right now consider TAT a "socket" whose exact contents will be finalized later; right now allow for Submission Time & Date, Day(s) & Hour countdown to deadline

Happy Path Flow:

  • User searches for patient by name and the right patient is returned

Alternative Flows:

  • Member not found

Open questions:

  • What about when there are multiple cases per member?

User story:
2.1 Ensure all required member demographic, contact, and coverage information is completed to allow Case Entry to proceed for an PA request.

Flow context:
This is step 1 of 5 in the Intake flow: 

  1. Search for Patient
  2. Confirm Patient Info
    1. (2.1) Confirm Patient Info 
    2.  (2.2) Confirm Eligibility 1 
  3. Add Diagnosis
  4. Attach Clinical notes & documents 
  5. Add Complete Prover Info & Eligibility Check #2 

This is the very first step a user takes to intake/enter a case. 

Key interactions:

  • User can add to empty fields
  • User can edit filled fields (if data is wrong)

Happy Path Flow:

  • All information is pre-populated and, on visual review, correct 

Alternative Flows:

  • All information is pre-populated and, on visual review, user must make a few edits based on [fax]
  • NOT all information is pre-populated and user adds information based on [fax]

Open questions:

  • Does user have to manually save edits? Or is it saving in the background? 
  • If saving in the background we want some kind of visual feedback to indicate that's happening to user.

User Story
As a user I need to capture a valid ICD-10 diagnosis and cancer stage for the prior authorization (PA) case so the request can be processed, routed, and transmitted correctly, and so downstream treatment selection can be appropriately constrained.

Flow context:
This is step 1 of 5 in the Intake flow: 

  1. Search for Patient
  2. Confirm Patient Info
    1. (2.1) Confirm Patient Info 
    2.  (2.2) Confirm Eligibility 1 
  3. Add Diagnosis
    1. Diagnosis fields
    2. Ordering Provider name + office contact
  4. Attach Clinical notes & documents 
  5. Add Complete Prover Info & Eligibility Check #2 

This is the very first step a user takes to intake/enter a case. 

Key interactions:

  • User can search and select for ICD-10 code & description
  • User can select cancer stage (if required) 
  • User can add Ordering Provider Name + Office Contact info

Happy Path Flow:

  • User searches by ICD-10 code and/or diagnosis keywords. System returns matching ICD-10 options (filtered per rules). 
  • User selects an ICD-10.
  • User selects cancer stage (if required/available)

Alternative Flows:

  • User does not select one of the required fields. This error is highlighted and case cannot proceed. 

Open questions:

  • Does user have to manually save edits? Or is it saving in the background? 
  • If saving in the background we want some kind of visual feedback to indicate that's happening to user.

Screen Key:
