User story:
2.2. Determine whether the member has active health plan eligibility for the requested oncology treatment as of the planned treatment start date, in order to decide whether Case Entry may continue.

Flow context:
This is step 1 of 5 in the Intake flow: 

  1. Search for Patient
  2. Confirm Patient Info
    1.  (2.1)Confirm Patient Info 
    2.  (2.2) Confirm Eligibility #1 
  3. Add Diagnosis
  4. Attach Clinical notes & documents 
  5. Add Complete Prover Info & Eligibility Check #2 

This is the very first step a user takes to intake/enter a case. 

Key interactions:

  • User can click button to run 1st eligibility check 
  • User can ...

Happy Path Flow:

  • The system evaluates the member’s eligibility by comparing the planned Treatment Start Date to the member’s eligibility effective and expiration dates and enrollment information.

  • Eligibility is active for the Treatment Start Date.

  • The system allows Case Entry to proceed to scope validation.

Alternative Flows:

  • Exception Flows: If eligibility data is not available for evaluation, the system does not allow Case Entry to proceed beyond eligibility verification.

Open questions:

  • Error Messages: What are they for patient who is (a) not eligible or (b) no eligibility data available? 
  • This is eligibility check #1 so Treatment Start Date is not yet available, right?

fields.System.Description:
Component name: Inputs
Link to handover file:
https://www.figma.com/design/FeseK9eTADQklwYX6r6rdj/OncoHealth-Start-a-Prior-Auth?node-id=145-4489&t=18Vr1gtVY2vKn0JI-1

Context:
This component appeared across multiple screens in the Start a Prior Authorization Flow - Case Intake Screens Visual Treatment sessions. for information on design decision and

States and variants covered:

  • Sizes: two sizes (L and M)
  • States: default, hover, filled, focused, disabled, error, required

Token dependencies:

  • Using existing color tokens for background and border
  • Using existing typography tokens
  • Using existing spacing tokens
  • New token needed: alert-icon-size (currently hardcoded at 20px)

Open questions:

Under Success Criterion 2.4.11 (Focus Appearance)—which is a newer, more rigorous addition in WCAG 2.2—you have to ensure the focus indicator is clearly "perceivable." Here is how the rules actually apply to MUI inputs: 1. The "Area" Requirement The focus indicator must have an area at least as large as a 2 CSS pixel thick line along the shortest side of the element, OR a minimum area of pixels.

To achieve this, MUI increases the stroke from 1px to 2px on focus because it is the easiest way to mathematically guarantee you meet this area requirement.

We need to make sure we document for the dev/make them aware of the ARIA rules here.

. The "Disabled" Exemption As established, WCAG Success Criterion 1.4.3 grants a "free pass" to disabled elements. 2. The MUI Specifics MUI components generally apply aria-disabled="true" or the disabled attribute automatically when you pass the disabled prop. This is vital because:

  • Visuals alone aren't enough: If a sighted user sees greyed-out text, they know it's disabled. A screen reader user needs that programmatic state.
  • MUI Default Styling: Interestingly, MUI’s default theme for disabled text is usually around 0.38 opacity. I moved to .50, thus more legible than the standard Material Design spec.


Assigned to: @Andrii Zhdan

fields.System.Title: [BE] Apply Payer Restrictions on Diagnoses
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS selecting a diagnosis, I want the system to apply payer-specific restrictions on ICD-10 codes, so that I can only select diagnoses allowed for the member’s health plan.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should apply payer applicability rules before displaying ICD-10 search results:
  2. Payer applicability should be determined from the Payers configuration for the ICD-10 diagnosis in the Admin Portal.

  3. The system should filter the ICD-10 list based on the selected health plan and return only ICD-10 codes that:

    1. Are mapped to the selected Diagnosis Group, and
    2. Are configured as applicable to the selected health plan.
  4. When a health plan is selected, the system should:
    1. Include ICD-10 codes where the configured Payers list contains the selected health plan.
    2. Exclude ICD-10 codes where the configured Payers list does not contain the selected health plan.
  5. If an ICD-10 diagnosis is configured with all payer options selected, the system should treat it as applicable to all health plans.
  6. The system should return only ICD-10 codes with Active Status = true.
Considerations:
  • Health plan identifier used for filtering should be derived from member eligibility
  • If all payer options are selected, the system should interpret the diagnosis as applicable to all health plans.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Select Tx Type (Chemo vs GMT vs XRT)
fields.System.Description: As CRS team member, I want to select the assignment type for the case, so that the system understands what type of treatment is being requested before protocol details are entered.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. After Diagnosis selection is successfully completed in accordance with [V1.R1.] Diagnosis Selection & Details for Case , the system should allow the user to proceed to Treatment Type selection.

  2. The system should display the following Treatment Type options:

    1. Chemotherapy (Chemo):
      1. should be enabled and selectable for V1.R1.
    2. XRT (Radiation):
      1. should be disabled for V1.R1.
    3. GMT (Test):
      1. should be disabled for V1.R1.
  3. Each selectable option should correspond to a distinct Treatment Type value stored at the case level.
  4. The system should require selection of a Treatment Type before allowing navigation to treatment-specific details.
  5. The system should prevent the user from proceeding to any treatment workflow if no Treatment Type is selected.

  6. Upon selection of a Treatment Type, the system should:

    1. Persist the selected Treatment Type in the case context.
    2. Initialize the corresponding treatment workflow.
    3. Route the user to the appropriate treatment entry screen.
  7. The system should persist the selected Treatment Type when the case is saved in Draft status.
  8. The system should restore the previously selected Treatment Type when a Draft case is reopened.
Considerations:
  • Each Treatment Type should map to a distinct workflow configuration and associated data model.
  • Routing logic should be enforced server-side to prevent invalid state transitions.
  • Treatment Type should be stored as a controlled enumeration to support future expansion (e.g., Radiation, Test).

fields.System.Title: [FE] Enter dosing information for Ancillary Drugs
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want to enter dosing information for ancillary drugs so that required billing and treatment details are captured for review and processing.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Upon Ancillary drug(-і) addition, the system shouldЖ
    1. Render dosing tables based on the resolved Billing Method  in accordance with Separate Medical and Pharmacy Drugs into Distinct Dosing Tables 
    2. Display ancillary drugs in separate sections/tables by Clinical Category, where the section header displays the Clinical Category
  2. Upon rendering the dosing table(s), the system should:
    1. Populate the HCPCS code and drug name / description for each ancillary drug added.
    2. Allow selection of alternate drugs through the HCPCS code field dropdown in accordance with Allow Switching Between Equivalent Regimen Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI.
  3. For Ancillary Drugs Dosing Fields values, the system should pull forward any available default dosing values from Admin Portal.
    1. If no Ancillary Drugs default dosing values are available, the fields should remain blank.
  4. For Pharmacy Drugs:
    1. Calculate Pharmacy Quantity per Authorization 
    2. Select Drug Strength and Form
    3. Indicate when calculated Pharmacy Quantity per Authorization exceeds default
  5. For Medical Drugs:
    1. Calculate Medical Billing Units 
    2. Calculate Allowed Dose 
    3. Indicate when calculated Medical Billing Units exceed default.
  6. User should be able to enter values in all editable dosing fields:
    1. System-calculated fields and status fields should not be editable.
    2. Dosing guardrails, validation rules, and calculation logic should be applied consistently across Custom Regimens, predefined Regimens, and Ancillary Drugs in accordance with Allow User Edits to Dosing with Guardrails

fields.System.Title: [BE] Calculate Quantity per Cycle (Pharmacy  Drugs)
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want the system to calculate Quantity per Cycle for Pharmacy drugs so that the authorization reflects the correct dispensing quantity.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should calculate Quantity per Cycle only for drug rows where Billing Method = Pharmacy.
  2. The system should use the same Quantity per Cycle calculation logic for Pharmacy drugs added via Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, and Ancillary Drug.

  3. For Quantity per Cycle calculation to occur, the system should have the following available: 

    Dosage, Strength, Adm. Per Day, Adm. Days Per Cycle, Duration per AP/Cycle in Days.
  4. The system should calculate Quantity per Cycle as:
    1. (Dosage ÷ Strength) × Adm. Per Day × Adm. Days Per Cycle × Cycles.
  5. The system should round Quantity per Cycle to 2 decimal places using the same rounding rules defined for Allowed Dose.
  6. If any required field for the applicable calculation path is missing or invalid, the system should not perform the calculation
  7. If any required field for Quantity per Cycle is updated, then it should be re-calculated based on the new values.
Considerations:
  1. Will be calculated on FE, since it will require user input
  2. BE will need to validate + QA

fields.System.Title: [BE] Indicate When Quantity per Cycle Exceeds Default
fields.System.Description: As a CRS/CRR user, I want the system to flag Pharmacy drug rows when user-entered dosing changes cause Quantity per Cycle to exceed the default value, so that exceptions to the original regimen-derived dosing can be reviewed.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should calculate and retain a Default Quantity per Cycle value for each Pharmacy drug row populated from an Existing Regimen using the regimen default dosing values initially loaded for that row, in accordance with Calculate Quantity per Authorization (Pharmacy  Drugs)
  2. The system should recalculate the Current Quantity per Cycle value for the drug row when user edits change any input used in the calculation, including Dosage, Strength, Adm. Per Day, Adm. Days Per Cycle, and Cycles.
  3. The system should compare the Current Quantity per Cycle value to the retained Default Quantity per Cycle value for the same drug row and should mark the drug row as Exceeds Default when Current Quantity per Cycle> than Default Quantity per Cycle;
    1. The system should indicate when calculated Quantity per Cycle exceeds the default value.
    2. The system should make both values available for the affected drug row:
      1. Default Quantity per Cycle
      2. Current Quantity per Cycle
  4. The system should allow the user to enter an optional exceed reason for the affected drug row.
    1. The system should store the exceed reason at the individual case-drug-row level.
  5. The system should remove the Exceeds Default status automatically when the Current Quantity per Cycle value becomes less than or equal to the Default Quantity per Cycle value.
  6. The Exceeds Default evaluation should be performed independently for each drug row and should not affect other drug rows on the case.
  7. If either the Default Quantity per Cycle value or the Current Quantity per Cycle value cannot be calculated, the system should not evaluate Exceeds Default for that row.
  8. When the user proceeds with a Pharmacy drug row that remains in Exceeds Default status, the system should create a log record to capture, at minimum, the case identifier, drug row identifier, drug identifier/HCPCS, Default Quantity per Cycle, Current Quantity per Cycle, optional exceed reason, user identifier, and action timestamp.

fields.System.Title: Apply OOS Configuration from PDL During Chemo Entry
[FE] Change Benefit Type for Crossover Drug Before Case Submission
fields.System.Description: As a CSR/CRR, I want to change the selected benefit type for a crossover drug line while the case is in draft status, so that I can correct benefit routing and ensure the drug is evaluated using the correct benefit status before submission.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The user should be able to change the selected benefit type (Medical or Pharmacy) for a crossover drug line while the case is in draft status.
  2. When the user changes the selected benefit type, the system should remove the drug line from the current dosing table and the system should place the drug line into the corresponding dosing table.
  3. When the user changes the selected benefit type, the system should re-evaluate the applicable benefit status (medical vs pharmacy) and the system should re-derive current drug status immediately, including applying the Per Formulary defaulting rule when applicable.
  4. After case submission, the user should not be able to change the selected benefit type for crossover drug lines.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Search for a member using required inputs (guardrails)
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want to search for a member using strict, validated search field combinations, so that I can identify the correct member to start case entry, while only seeing members for the health plan(s) I’m authorized to access.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must provide a Member Search capability prior to new case creation
  2. The system must uniquely identify exactly one member entity prior to allowing progression in Case Entry.
    1. For R1, all authenticated users have access to all configured Health Plans.

  3. Search execution must require one of the following combinations:

    1. Health Plan + Date of Birth + Member ID
    2. Health Plan + Date of Birth + First Name + Last Name
    3. Health Plan + Date of Birth + Member ID + First Name + Last Name
  4. The system must validate all search fields in accordance with the Data Dictionary and block execution if validation fails.
  5. The system must enforce payer-specific configuration for Member ID for the selected Health Plan, including:
    1. Max Member ID Length (Required in Payer Configs)
    2. Member ID Expression / Pattern (Optional in Payer Configs)
    3. Member ID Message  (Optional in Payer Configs)
  6. The system must execute the search strictly within the selected Health Plan 
  7. Matching must be exact; when multiple identifiers are provided, all identifiers must match the same member entity.
  8. The system must return Found when exactly one distinct member entity matches the criteria.
  9. The system must return Not Found when no member or more than one distinct member matches the criteria
    1. Edge cases related to Member Search
  10. The system must log each search attempt

Technical Considerations:
  1. The eligibility ingestion and ETL pipeline are out of scope for NF Data team. Member Search consumes eligibility data as provided by the OH data team.
  2. Search must return a single logical member entity, even if multiple eligibility records exist behind it.
  3. No identity reconciliation or record merging is performed in R1.
  4. Configs (from OADB) are out of scope for this story > stub for fetching the configs

fields.System.Title: [FE] View/Edit Member Information Form for Found Member (core eligibility data + optional eligibility attributes )
fields.System.Description:
As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to retrieve and present member information after a member is found,
So that I can review demographic, contact, eligibility, and basic treatment context before proceeding with case entry decisions.

Story Scope / Boundaries:
  • Triggered when Member Search returns Found (single logical member)
  • Retrieves member -related data associated with the identified member
  • Makes member information available for review and limited modification where permitted
  • Does not create a draft case
  • Does not perform eligibility-at-time-of-service validation or scope determination (handled by subsequent stories)
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must render the Member Information form after a successful Member Search result (Found).
  2. The form must display member information organized into logical groups as defined in the Data Dictionary:
    1. Demographic information
    2. Member  contact information
    3. Member representative information (when available)
    4. Eligibility information - all required fields
    5. Basic treatment information (limited to treatment start date)
  3. All eligibility-derived member information must be displayed as read-only.
  4. The system must not allow editing, correction, or override of eligibility-derived data in R1.
  5. Treatment Start Date must be displayed as empty by default:
    1. The system must allow the user to enter or modify Treatment Start Date.
  6. Displaying the Member Information form must not create, persist, or update a case record.
  7. If required eligibility attributes (as defined in the Data Dictionary) are missing, the system must prevent progression to downstream steps and display a blocking message - see Edge Cases for Display Member Information
  8. If optional eligibility attributes (as defined in the Data Dictionary) are missing, then:

    1. op

      tional attributes are not displayed or displayed blank per UI design
    2. do not block progression

fields.System.Title: [FE] Validate Member Eligibility at Treatment Start Date
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to validate that the member has active coverage on the treatment start date, so that only eligible cases can proceed.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Eligibility verification may be initiated only after:
    1. A member has been successfully identified, and
    2. Patient Information has been saved with a Treatment Start Date.
  2. Treatment Start Date is the authoritative reference date for eligibility verification.

  3. Upon initiation, the system must evaluate whether the member has coverage active on the Treatment Start Date using: 

    Coverage Effective Date, Coverage Expiration Date
  4. If multiple eligibility records exist for the member under the selected Health Plan, the system must evaluate all returned eligibility records.

  5. An eligibility record is considered active if:

    1. Treatment Start Date is greater than or equal to Coverage Effective Date, and
    2. Treatment Start Date is less than or equal to Coverage Expiration Date
  6. If at least one eligibility record is active on the Treatment Start Date, the system must return outcome: Eligible.
  7. If no eligibility record is active on the Treatment Start Date, the system must return outcome: Not Eligible.

  8. If the outcome is Not Eligible, the system must:

    1. Prevent progression to scope determination, and
    2. Block further progression in the case entry flow
    3. Edge Cases – Eligibility Verification
  9. The system records the eligibility verification outcome and the eligibility record(s) evaluated for audit.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Create Draft Case and Save Progress
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want to create a draft case and save my progress during case entry, so that I can either pause and resume later or continue directly with diagnosis and treatment selection.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must allow Draft Case creation once a member has been successfully identified and the Member Information form is rendered in accordance with:
    1. Search for a member using required inputs (guardrails)
    2. View/Edit Member Information Form for Found Member (core eligibility data)

  2. Draft Case creation must not require: 

    Eligibility verification and Scope determination

  3. The system must provide two actions:

    1. Save as Draft and remain on the current step.
    2. Save as Draft and proceed to the next step:
      1. Eligibility verification is required

  4. Upon Draft Case creation, the system must:

    1. Generate and assign a unique Case Identifier.
    2. Persist all data captured to date.
    3. Set Case Status to Draft.
    4. Record Created Date/Time and Created By.
  5. The system must allow saving the Draft throughout case entry.
  6. The user must be able to retrieve and resume the Draft Case at any time.
  7. All Draft Case creation and updates must be logged for audit.
Considerations:
  1. Case State Model > Draft:
    1. Created after member selection.
    2. May contain incomplete data.
    3. Eligibility and scope may be pending or not evaluated.
    4. User may edit and save repeatedly.

fields.System.Title: [Data] Spike: Admin Portal (AP) Drug API Coverage & Clinical Data Model Validation for NewUM R1 (ICD-10, Drugs, Regimens)
fields.System.Description:
Objectives:


Confirm that Admin Portal (Drug API) contains and exposes all required clinical data for:
  1. [V1.R1.] Diagnosis Selection & Details for Case
  2. [V1.R1.] Main Regimen Selection
  3. [V1.R1.] Allow Custom Regimens
  4. [V1.R1.] Select Ancillary Drugs
  5. [V1.R1.] Capture Basic Dosing Details
  6. [V1.R1.] Apply Payer Drug Configurations
  7. [V1.R1.] Apply Payer Non-Preferred Drug Rules
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
The investigation should cover:

  1. ICD 10 Data:
    1. Availability of ICD-10 codes and descriptions
    2. Diagnosis Group mapping
    3. Cancer stage configuration 
    4. Treatment type applicability (Chemo / GMT / XRT)
    5. Payer-specific ICD logic (common vs payer-extended codes)
  2. Master Drug + PDL (Payer Drug Library) Overlay Model
    1. Drug identifiers (NDC / GPI / HCPCS etc.):
      1. Brand/Generic Drug Name
      2. Alternative Drugs
      3. Ancillary Drugs
    2. EADL flags (if applicable)
    3. Dosing Metadata
      1. Default dosing parameters
      2. Calculation logic (BSA, flat dose, etc.)
  3. Regimen Library
    1. Regimen → Diagnosis Group linkage
    2. Regimen drug composition
Expected Deliverables
  1. Confirm whether AP can serve as the single source of truth for clinical + payer configuration
  2. Identify gaps in API coverage or data modeling
  3. Clarify rule execution ownership (AP vs NewUM)
  4. Recommend architecture approach (direct API vs cache/hybrid)
  5. Highlight R1 risks and ERD impacts

fields.System.Title: [FE] Select Cancer Stage for Diagnosis

fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should require an ICD-10 diagnosis to be selected before a Cancer Stage value can be entered:
    1. Data Dictionary
  2. The system should limit Cancer Stage options to values relevant to the selected diagnosis (Diagnosis Group).
  3. The system should require Cancer Stage selection before the user can proceed to the next workflow step.
  4. The system should allow selection of exactly one Cancer Stage value.
  5. The system should save the selected Cancer Stage on the case.
  6. If the user changes the selected ICD-10 diagnosis, the system should clear the previously selected Cancer Stage.
  7. After ICD-10 change, the system should require Cancer Stage to be selected again before proceeding.

fields.System.Title: [BE] SPIKE: Investigate Payer applicability
fields.System.Description: https://oncologyanalytics.visualstudio.com/newUM/_workitems/edit/183544
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Scope

  1. Understand how payer applicability for ICD-10 diagnoses is stored and exposed from the Admin Portal data model.
  2. Confirm how “all payer options selected” is represented in source data and how it should be interpreted in NewUM.
  3. Determine which health plan identifier from member eligibility should be used for filtering.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Select Tx Type (Chemo vs GMT vs XRT)
fields.System.Description: As CRS team member, I want to select the assignment type for the case, so that the system understands what type of treatment is being requested before protocol details are entered.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. After Diagnosis selection is successfully completed in accordance with [V1.R1.] Diagnosis Selection & Details for Case , the system should allow the user to proceed to Treatment Type selection.

  2. The system should display the following Treatment Type options:

    1. Chemotherapy (Chemo):
      1. should be enabled and selectable for V1.R1.
    2. XRT (Radiation):
      1. should be disabled for V1.R1.
    3. GMT (Test):
      1. should be disabled for V1.R1.
  3. Each selectable option should correspond to a distinct Treatment Type value stored at the case level.
  4. The system should require selection of a Treatment Type before allowing navigation to treatment-specific details.
  5. The system should prevent the user from proceeding to any treatment workflow if no Treatment Type is selected.

  6. Upon selection of a Treatment Type, the system should:

    1. Persist the selected Treatment Type in the case context.
    2. Initialize the corresponding treatment workflow.
    3. Route the user to the appropriate treatment entry screen.
  7. The system should persist the selected Treatment Type when the case is saved in Draft status.
  8. The system should restore the previously selected Treatment Type when a Draft case is reopened.
Considerations:
  • Each Treatment Type should map to a distinct workflow configuration and associated data model.
  • Routing logic should be enforced server-side to prevent invalid state transitions.
  • Treatment Type should be stored as a controlled enumeration to support future expansion (e.g., Radiation, Test).

See Configuration Management - NewUM Product Team - Confluence or additional context
Requirements:

  • All changes have to be audited according to NFR, requirements extracted to distinct ticket  for it #184180
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Create new or update existing DB structure(s) to hold JSON configuration files

    3. Feel free to add other entities necessary for technical implementation (e.g enumerators, lookup tables etc.)
  2. Create APIs to enable NewUM workflows and configuration FE to consume and manage data
    1. APIs for management 
      1. Accessible only to users in OH Configuration Admin Role (exact role name TBD)
      2. OH Config admins should be able to view and update all configurations for all payers, and create new ones
      3. No delete endpoint 
    2.  APIs for NewUM
      1. These will be used by NewUM to fetch configurations on runtime 
      2. Read only, no create/edit/delete endpoints needed
      3. General level configurations available to everyone
      4. Payer level configuration - endpoint returns configuration only for a payer that is selected on case entry
  3. Validations:
    1. Only 1 record with combination of values Type+Grain Level+Grain Value+Configuration Name can be active at any given time (check ValidTo and ValidFrom dates and status)

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create Rules Repository
fields.System.Description:
See https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5140742173/DRAFT+Configuration+Guide#Rules for additional context

  • We need to be able to save various rules that will handle how NewUM behaves for particular payers or use-cases
    • This rule configuration repository will not be consumed by any other system
  • Access to rule configurations should be restricted, with only dedicated OH configuration administrator being able to make updates
  • While building dedicated user friendly UI to manage rules and configurations is OOS for phase 1 of the project, when designing this solution attention should be given that UI features can be implemented in the future without need for significant refactoring.
    • NewUM configurations and rules need to be readable and manageable by a non-engineer, i.e. changes in configuration and updating of the rules should not require code changes and don’t rely on SDLC.
  • NewUM rules configuration repository should support:
    • Rule execution setup
      • Defining workflow, steps and events that rule needs to trigger on
        • Workflow configuration is OOS of this ticket, there is an explicit ticket for that #184319
    • Defining rule tiers
      • 3 tiers (1,2,3)
    • Payer specific configuration and behavior feature flags
      • Business rules related settings

      • These can be drilled down further to LoB level->Product line level->Employer group level

    • Defining conditions rule is checking, result of the check is always SUCCESS or FAIL
      • Multiple conditions can be nested
    • Defining actions that need to be taken on each result
      • Multiple actions can be specified
    • Rule configurations are hierarchical (grain levels)

      • Even tho some configuration level can be shared (e.g. LoBs), each configuration/rule should be tied to specific payer org they apply to

    • Users need to be able to configure hierarchy of grain levels (parent-child relationships) on payer level
      • This is OOS of this ticket and handled explicitly by #184321
      • Lowest grain level configuration always takes precedence
    • Effective Dating
      • Valid from and Valid to metadata parameters that rules engine will consume in order to determine should rule be triggered
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Create new or update existing DB structure(s)
    1. Must support following data fields:

      Column name

      Description

      Notes

      ID

      UUID

       

      Type

      Type of rule, used for classification, can contain multiple values with delimiter

       

      Workflow

      Business process workflow is being used to, e.g. “PA”, “Appeals” etc.

      For v1, only 1 workflow type is needed = “PA”

      Rule Name

      Rule name

       should be unique on workflow-level-step-event level

      Display Name

       Rule name to be displayed to end user

      e.g in FE editor, rule execution logs etc. 

      Rule description

      Description of the rule

       

      Level

      Level (grain) on which rule takes affect

      Rules can be nested e.g.

      Level= payer
      Level= payer/LoB/Product Line

      allowed values = https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5140742173/DRAFT+Configuration+Guide#Grain-Level

      Level Values

      Specific level values rule applies for

       e.g. if level is employer group, this column would contain employer group names to which configuration applies for

      Tier

      Rule tier

       values from https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5140742173/DRAFT+Configuration+Guide#Tiers

      Step

      Step in which rule should be triggered

       Allowed values are from workflow configuration, read from #184319

      Event

      Event within step which should trigger the event

      Allowed values https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5140742173/DRAFT+Configuration+Guide#Event 

      Conditions

      Conditions rule should test when triggered. Multiple conditions can be nested using logic operators (AND, OR, NOR) and basic operators (<, >, =, contains..)

       

      Action

      Action(s) that should be triggered on SUCCESS and FAIL result.

       

      Status

      Status of the rule record

       Allowed values https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5127569409/Rules+and+configurations+functional+specification#Rule-and-Configuration-Lifecycle

      ValidFrom

      Date record is valid from

       

      ValidTo

      Date record is valid to

       


    2. Feel free to add other entities necessary for technical implementation (e.g enumerators, lookup tables etc.)
  2. Create APIs to enable FE to consume and manage data
    1. Read endpoint available to everyone with active NewUM access
    2. Create & Update endpoints available only to users in OH Admin role (role name TBC)
    3. No delete endpoint
  3. Validations:
    1. Only 1 record with combination of values Workflow+Rule Name+Level can be active at any given time (check ValidTo and ValidFrom dates and status)
    2. Mandatory: ID, Workflow, Rule Name, Tier, Step, Event, Conditions, Status, ValidFrom
    3. If Level is not empty, than  Level Value is mandatory
  4. All changes have to be audited according to NFR, special ticket for that #184183

  • updating out-of-date or incorrect eligibility records from the UI

Write unit tests exercising basic CRUD functionality for eligibility.   

See the Data subsystem of this draft architecture diagram.

fields.System.Title: Create Case domain around Case schema
fields.System.Description:
This module is one of the trickiest to write. Much of the system will depend on its correctness, and it will eventually need to manage:
  • write-locked, denormalized case schema (potentially stored as SQLite blobs)
  • Case Meta Schema in OLTP DB
  • case data in the Reporting OLAP DB (later)
Case data may come from:
  • the UI via the API gateway
  • bulk ingest from migration pipeline
  • rules inference
Each of these sources corresponds to separately-deployed code that could call this module. Because each case SQLite will be write-locked, there may be contention. This module will need to coexist with instances of itself running on different machines. 

When eligibility records update, the Member Admin module will need to call this module in order to add new eligibility records to related open cases. 

Not all of these problems have to be solved now, but the interface should be sufficient to hide the solutions. 

See the Data subsystem of this draft architecture diagram.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• DAL can be configured to read/write SQLite files from blob storage or the local filesystem, similar to how OLTP can either be local or remote
  • unit tests for operations listed in description
  • underlying schema is treated as write-only
  • get review of interface from @Jack Hall

    1. An eligibility result is displayed to the user.
    2. Only the successful (happy path) outcome is supported.

fields.System.Title: Set up Databricks for Migration Pipeline
fields.System.Description:
The Migration Pipeline will draw data from the legacy data stores (OADB, EligibilityMS, ProviderMS and DrugsMS) in order to populate NewUM with data. For Phase 1, start with eligibility data. Cases and drug elements are next; @Cory Johnson is the SME for that. Build some familiarity with the legacy schemas. 

Because the core backend has not yet set up a bulk ingest API, simply put any output data in a delta table for now.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• Databricks workspace configured
  • service principal created
  • A skeleton pipeline that queries any sample of records from the OADB (test environment is ok) and writes the results to a delta table
  • recommended but not yet required: deploy pipeline using a Databricks Asset Bundle for IaC

fields.System.Title: Design starting data model for thin slice
fields.System.Description: Case schema should be denormalized, with normalized Case Meta and Eligibility schemas.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• Case Meta and Eligibility schemas defined. 
  • Neither needs to be complete but should be enough to support the thin vertical slice. 
  • New schemas are separate from the denormalized Case schema.
